title: Sharm El Sheikh Travel Guide: Explore Egypt’s Red Sea Jewel

sections.Explore Egypt’s Red Sea Jewel:
Sharm El Sheikh Travel Guide: Explore Egypt’s Red Sea Jewel
Sharm El Sheikh, located at the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula, is one of Egypt’s most iconic Red Sea destinations , famous for its luxurious resorts, stunning beaches, and world-class diving opportunities. Known as the “Pearl of the Red Sea,” Sharm El Sheikh attracts travelers from around the globe seeking adventure, relaxation, and unforgettable natural beauty.
At Egypt Travel Guide , we specialize in helping travelers plan their perfect Egyptian experience. From luxury accommodations to personalized excursions and diving adventures, we make exploring Sharm El Sheikh seamless and memorable.
Discover Sharm El Sheikh: Diving and Snorkeling Paradise
Sharm El Sheikh is recognized worldwide for its diving and snorkeling hotspots. The Red Sea in this region boasts vibrant coral reefs and abundant marine life, including tropical fish, turtles, and dolphins. Notable dive sites include:
● Ras Mohammed National Park: Egypt’s most famous marine park, offering dramatic underwater landscapes and thriving coral gardens.
● Shark and Yolanda Reef: Popular for drift diving and encounters with colorful marine species.
● Tiran Island: Known for crystal-clear waters and spectacular coral walls, ideal for both snorkeling and diving enthusiasts.
Whether you are a professional diver or a first-time snorkeler, the underwater world of Sharm El Sheikh promises unforgettable experiences for every traveler.
Relax on Stunning Beaches and Coastal Resorts
Sharm El Sheikh is not just about diving — it is also home to some of Egypt’s most beautiful beaches. Areas like Naama Bay and Shark Bay offer pristine sands, turquoise waters, and family-friendly resorts. For travelers seeking a quieter experience, Ras Um Sid and Nabq Bay provide tranquil beaches perfect for relaxation, snorkeling, or simply soaking up the sun.
Adventure Beyond the Sea
The Sinai desert surrounding Sharm El Sheikh offers exciting excursions for adventure seekers. From camel rides and quad biking to desert safaris, visitors can enjoy breathtaking sunsets and explore the natural desert landscape. Trips to Mount Sinai and St. Catherine’s Monastery offer cultural and historical experiences that complement the coastal attractions, making your journey even more enriching.
Best Time to Visit Sharm El Sheikh
The ideal period to visit Sharm El Sheikh is from October to April , when the weather is moderate and ideal for beach activities, diving, and desert excursions. Summer months are hotter, making water activities more appealing while land tours may require early mornings or evenings to avoid peak temperatures.
Plan Your Sharm El Sheikh Trip with Egypt Travel Guide at Egypt Travel Guide,
we help you design the perfect itinerary for Sharm El Sheikh . Whether you want a luxury resort stay, guided diving tours, desert adventures, or cultural excursions, our team ensures every detail is customized to your preferences. We also provide insider tips on the best beaches, snorkeling spots, and dining experiences to make your trip unforgettable. Explore the beauty and adventure of Sharm El Sheikh with Egypt Travel Guide by your side, and create memories that last a lifetime.

title: Marsa Alam Travel Guide: Discover Egypt’s Hidden Red Sea Paradise

sections.Marsa Alam Travel Guide: Discover Egypt’:
Marsa Alam, located on Egypt’s southern Red Sea coast, is a hidden gem for travelers seeking pristine beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and unforgettable adventures . Often overlooked compared to Hurghada or Sharm El-Sheikh, Marsa Alam offers a unique blend of tranquility, natural beauty, and cultural experiences that make it an ideal destination for families, solo travelers, and adventure enthusiasts alike.
At Egypt Travel Guide , we specialize in creating personalized travel plans that help you explore Marsa Alam seamlessly. From arranging accommodations to booking diving excursions and desert tours, we ensure your trip is both stress-free and unforgettable.
Explore the Red Sea: Diving and Snorkeling in Marsa Alam
Marsa Alam is renowned for its world-class diving and snorkeling sites. Its reefs are home to an astonishing variety of marine life, including green turtles, dolphins, and dugongs. Some of the most popular spots include:
● Elphinstone Reef: Famous for its steep walls, abundant coral gardens, and frequent sightings of reef sharks.
● Abu Dabbab Bay: Known as the “House of Turtles,” this bay is perfect for snorkeling and spotting dugongs in calm, clear waters.
● Dolphin House (Sha‘ab Samadai): Offers a rare chance to swim alongside playful dolphins in their natural habitat.
Whether you are an experienced diver or a first-time snorkeler, the underwater world of Marsa Alam provides a truly unforgettable experience.
Relax on Marsa Alam’s Pristine Beaches
Marsa Alam is also celebrated for its quiet, unspoiled beaches, ideal for relaxation and water activities. Beaches like Sharm El-Luli and Coraya Beach offer soft white sand, turquoise waters, and excellent snorkeling opportunities right from the shore. For eco-conscious travelers, the nearby Hamata Reserve and Qulaan Islands provide a peaceful escape surrounded by nature.
Desert Adventures and Unique Experiences
Beyond the coastline, Marsa Alam’s desert landscapes are equally captivating. Visitors can explore Wadi El-Gemal National Park, which is rich in wildlife, desert scenery, and ancient rock art. Desert safaris, camel rides, and Bedouin cultural experiences allow travelers to witness stunning sunsets and the starry night sky, creating memories that last a lifetime.
Combine Nature with Culture
Marsa Alam’s location also makes it an excellent base for cultural and historical excursions. Day trips to Luxor or the Temple of Seti I at Khanais allow travelers to experience Egypt’s ancient heritage alongside the natural beauty of the Red Sea . This combination of adventure, relaxation, and history ensures a well-rounded travel experience.
Best Time to Visit Marsa Alam
The ideal months to visit Marsa Alam are October to April, when temperatures are moderate and sea conditions are perfect for diving and snorkeling. While summer months are warmer and suitable for sunbathing, planning your trip during the cooler months ensures a more comfortable and enjoyable experience.
Let Egypt Travel Guide Plan Your Trip
At Egypt Travel Guide , we are committed to making your Marsa Alam travel experience seamless and memorable. We provide expert guidance on accommodations, diving trips, desert excursions, and local dining experiences, ensuring every detail is tailored to your needs. Whether it’s arranging guided tours or giving insider tips on the best beaches and snorkeling spots, our team is here to help you make the most of your visit. Discover the unspoiled beauty of Marsa Alam and create memories that will last a lifetime. With Egypt Travel Guide by your side, your Red Sea adventure will be effortless, enjoyable, and unforgettable.

title: Hurghada Travel Guide – Discover Egypt’s Premier Red Sea Destination

sections.Hurghada Travel Guide – Discover Egypt’s:
Hurghada is one of Egypt’s most renowned coastal cities, located along the western shore of the Red Sea. Known for its crystal-clear waters, vibrant coral reefs, luxury resorts, and diverse excursions, Hurghada has become a top choice for international travelers seeking relaxation, adventure, and authentic Egyptian experiences. From underwater exploration to desert safaris, Hurghada offers a complete holiday experience for couples, families, and solo travelers.
Why Visit Hurghada, Egypt?
Hurghada combines natural beauty with modern tourism infrastructure, making it one of the most accessible and enjoyable destinations in Egypt.
● Exceptional Red Sea Beaches with calm, warm waters
● World-class snorkeling and diving sites international resorts and family-friendly hotels
● Easy access via Hurghada International Airport
● Wide range of excursions and activities
Its year-round sunshine and welcoming atmosphere make Hurghada suitable for both short stays and extended vacations.
Top Things to Do in Hurghada Red Sea
Snorkeling and Scuba Diving
Hurghada is internationally recognized for its coral reefs and marine biodiversity. The Red Sea offers excellent visibility, colorful coral formations, and diverse sea life, making it ideal for both beginner and advanced divers. Popular dive areas include shallow reefs, shipwrecks, and protected marine parks. Giftun Island and Mahmya Island Excursions A boat trip to Giftun Island is one of the most popular activities in Hurghada. These protected islands feature white sandy beaches, turquoise waters, and outstanding snorkeling opportunities. Mahmya Island, in particular, is known for its well-maintained facilities and pristine environment.
Desert Safari Adventures
Beyond the coastline, Hurghada’s surrounding desert offers exciting safari experiences. Visitors can enjoy quad biking, 4×4 jeep safaris, camel rides, and traditional Bedouin dinners, providing insight into local desert life and culture.
Hurghada Marina
Hurghada Marina is a modern waterfront area featuring restaurants, cafés, shops, and evening entertainment. It is an ideal place for dining, relaxing, and enjoying views of luxury yachts and the Red Sea at sunset.
Hurghada Grand Aquarium
The Hurghada Grand Aquarium is a popular family attraction showcasing a wide range of marine species, including sharks, rays, turtles, and colorful fish. It also features themed exhibits and educational displays, making it suitable for visitors of all ages. Hurghada Museum For travelers interested in culture and history, the Hurghada Museum presents artifacts from ancient Egyptian, Greco-Roman, Islamic, and modern periods, offering a cultural perspective alongside beach activities.
Best Time to Visit Hurghada Hurghada
Enjoys a warm desert climate throughout the year.
● October to April: Ideal weather for sightseeing, diving, and outdoor activities
● May to September: Hotter temperatures, perfect for beach holidays and water activities.
Thanks to its stable climate, Hurghada remains a popular destination in all seasons.
Accommodation in Hurghada
Hurghada offers a wide range of accommodation options, including luxury resorts, beachfront hotels, family-friendly properties, and budget-friendly stays. Many resorts provide all-inclusive packages, private beaches, swimming pools, and direct access to water sports facilities.
Food and Dining Experience
Visitors to Hurghada can enjoy both international cuisine and traditional Egyptian dishes. Fresh seafood is widely available, along with popular local meals such as koshari, grilled meats, and Middle Eastern specialties. Restaurants range from resort dining to local cafés and marina restaurants.
Practical Travel Tips
● Currency: Egyptian Pound (EGP)
● Language: Arabic, with English widely spoken in tourist areas
● Transportation: Taxis, hotel transfers, and private tours are commonly used
● Water: Bottled water is recommended for drinking
● Tipping: Common practice in hotels, restaurants, and guided services

title: Souvenirs and Shopping in Egypt

sections.Haggling Is Part of the Experience!:
What would a holiday be without bringing a piece of it home? In Egypt, shopping is more than a transaction—it’s an experience shaped by culture, conversation, and a touch of strategy. One phrase you’ll hear often is: “Did you negotiate a good price?” In many local markets, bargaining is not only expected, it’s part of the enjoyment. Those who engage with it tend to walk away with better deals—and memorable stories.
A Shopper’s Landscape
Shopping in Egypt is remarkably accessible and varied. Even travelers who arrive with no intention to buy often leave with something in hand. Hotel boutiques offer convenience and a quick solution for essentials, but prices there are typically higher than in city centers. For better value and a more authentic experience, it’s worth stepping beyond the hotel.
Across destinations like Hurghada , you’ll find a wide range of options—from modern retail outlets to traditional bazaars. Over the past two decades, these tourist hubs have evolved significantly, now combining international-style shopping with deeply rooted local traditions.
The Bazaar Experience
Traditional markets remain at the heart of Egypt’s shopping culture. In these vibrant spaces, you may be welcomed with a glass of hibiscus tea—known locally as Karkadeh—while browsing handmade goods, textiles, perfumes, spices, and souvenirs. Vendors are often charismatic and persuasive, each presenting their products as the best available. It’s not unusual to hear that a rare item can be sourced “just nearby” from a relative’s shop.
This is where negotiation comes in. Bargaining is not confrontational—it’s a social interaction. Prices are flexible, and the process itself builds rapport. That said, if you feel uncomfortable at any point, it’s perfectly acceptable to walk away. There are always other दुक shops and opportunities.
Modern Retail and Local Markets
For those who prefer fixed prices and a more structured shopping environment, large centers such as Senzo Mall offer a familiar retail experience. Here you’ll find everything from fashion brands to electronics and travel gear. These centers are ideal for quick, hassle-free purchases.
At the same time, local markets provide a deeper cultural insight. Visiting a fish market near the Hurghada Marina or a traditional fruit and vegetable souk offers a glimpse into everyday Egyptian life—colorful, busy, and full of energy.
Payments and Practical Tips
In most tourist areas, major currencies such as Euros and US Dollars are widely accepted. However, smaller shops and markets often prefer the local currency, the Egyptian pound. Currency exchange is straightforward, with banks and ATMs readily available. Credit and debit cards are commonly accepted in larger stores and malls.
A few practical tips for a smoother experience:
Check the current exchange rate before making purchases.
Expect fixed prices in malls, but negotiate in smaller shops.
Keep small bills handy for convenience.
Approach bargaining with a friendly attitude—it’s part of the culture, not a conflict.
A Cultural Takeaway
Shopping in Egypt blends commerce with connection. While modern retail offers efficiency, the traditional markets provide something less tangible but more memorable: human interaction. Whether you leave with handcrafted souvenirs, spices, or simply a good story, the experience itself becomes part of your journey.

title: Illness, Medication and Medical Help

sections.Illness, Medication and Medical Help:
It’s very important and common question when traveling: What is the medical care and services in the place I will be staying at?
You must have already heard, guests planning a trip to Egypt are worried mostly about diarrhea asking: Should we take the medication with us or get vaccinated? Let us speak about it:
Our advice:
Yes, you are flying to Africa, but not central Africa, where people may contract malaria and other worrying diseases. You will not find any special warning for Egypt and you do not need to be vaccinated. If you already have minor issues (stomach pain, diarrhea, ear infection, skin allergy... etc.), you will find many pharmacies where you can buy everything without a prescription. The *diarrhea medications* are Antinal, Drotazide or Diax tablets. Children and pregnant women are also safe with Antinal. The little ones take this as syrup. However, medication should not be taken in advance, as a “booster”.
The biggest problem are always intestinal colds. What does that mean? Tourists are spending time on sunny beach - outside in the heat, enter lobbies and restaurants where is too cold, refreshing themselves with ice cold drinks. The AC in guests’ rooms is running in full, because anybody has time to set it lower every time he / she comes back. There is colorful buffet with so many different foodies and treats waiting at the end of the day…
The first and most important advice is: Never ever travel without travel insurance! Obligatory EU or UK insurance does not apply here if you do not have extra commercial insurance for abroad /outside Europe/. Please don’t rely on “I never needed that.” You should know that medical treatment abroad is not cheap. If you are treated somewhere / in the hotel clinic, in the hospital / on an outpatient basis, you pay the bill and your health insurance company will refund you back in your home country. According to the insurance companies’ contracts, the insurance covers the costs for inpatient treatment directly (We recommend you to read the insurance contract properly and declaring your health condition as per your GP). Most hotels have their hotel ambulance and a doctor for first aid. For major injuries you will find private hospitals with everything you need for treatment, many of European standards.
If you need to take your regular medications, make sure you have a supply for your entire stay. If you are taking any medication that is not legal or banned in Egypt, you must also have an official prescription from your doctor with you. The medicines brought into the country are limited “for personal use only.”
We wish you a nice holiday without any health problems!

According to the insurance companies’ contracts, the insurance covers the costs for inpatient treatment directly (We recommend you to read the insurance contract properly and declaring your health condition as per your GP). Most hotels have their hotel ambulance and a doctor for first aid. For major injuries you will find private hospitals with everything you need for treatment, many of European standards.
If you need to take your regular medications, make sure you have a supply for your entire stay. If you are taking any medication that is not legal or banned in Egypt, you must also have an official prescription from your doctor with you. The medicines brought into the country are limited “for personal use only.”
We wish you a nice holiday without any health problems!

title: Egypt : Time To Discover

sections.Egypt Is Not Just Hotel Story:
Once you have already decided to travel to Egypt, sure you want to know which options you have for spending your free and valuable vacation time.
We don't want to disrupt your action plan consisting of getting enough sleep and relax, but rest assured that relaxation can also be very active.
As far as security is concerned, we have already said that you do not need to take any special precautions here. You can always spend quality time with your partner, friends or family.
And we know that Egypt is not the hotel complex. Egypt comes to you just behind the hotel gate and the tourist center.
Our advice:
In general, Egypt has three major values: history, desert and sea . It offers you stable warm weather (exceptionally windy days with sandstorms) all year round and it's up to you to enjoy it to the maximum.
Worth to mention that you can also fall in love with Egyptian cuisine and friendly people…
As Egyptian history is rich and interesting, take the opportunity to see some sights. Whether you dream of visiting Pyramids or the Valley of the Kings. You will find hundreds of offers to get there. Good guides and providers will give you useful tips (not only) how to survive long hours under the hot sun.
Clothing
As also recommended in our travel offer, you should not underestimate the climate which you have reached here. Be ready with the clothes that protect your body from the sunburns (although we know it's not always 100%).
The material of your clothes should be light and airy. You might not understand at first why Egyptians use to wear long sleeves even in summer. It's about everyday life in the heat.
For long outdoor activities, we recommend you (kids and adults) to cover your head. It is a must on the sightseeing tours. You will always appreciate a headscarf in the desert – against the sand and against the wind.
Drinking regime (and food structure)
It's not just a phrase to keep yourself hydrated. The body needs more fluids than normal and you should take care of getting them. Of course, getting the right ones is important. The hot weather calls for something cold and refreshing to drink. The glass of cold cola, beer, juice...
You will be surprised again that the Egyptians refresh themselves with the hot drink even in hot weather. How can you accept something hot when you feel like your body is about to explode?
It's really very simple. The hot drink does not cause a temperature shock. In turn, it prepares the body to receive something colder afterwards.
Note that temperature shock is most often the cause of stomach problems. If you additionally include all the dishes from the buffet tables....
It is clear that the best refreshment is the water or drink enriched with (for example) lemon.
If you get a chance to try the local food, it's definitely worth it. Very simple but also very tasty! You can always try a local restaurant or cook something from famous dishes (inspired by our article about Egyptian food).
Hotel name
This might sounds a bit crazy, but sometimes problems can arise when you go on tour and don't remember the exact name of your hotel. Yes, that really happens. There are many hotel chains in the city, hotel names are changing and not all (taxi) drivers are updated!
It is not enough to say "Sunrise", "Albatros" or Hilton Hotel. There are hotels in different areas. If you're not sure what the exact name is, you can end up at the location, which the driver mostly knows. Not once the guest of the Hilton Resort ended up in the Hilton Long Beach or Hilton Plaza (completely different direction, but you don't know that at first). Sunrise Holidays has a completely different location than Sunrise Garden Beach...
You can rent the car and drive yourself with the international driving license. Even you will see from the start that driving here has different rules than in Europe….
Photographs
Usually you want to keep your travel experience alive in the photos. In Egypt it's very easy to connect to social networks, so it's time to ...
You get beautiful shots from the desert and the safari . You will be amazed by the natural beauty of the Red Sea (Orange Bay, Mahmya, Paradise). You can get stunning shots from ordinary Egyptian life.
Keep in mind, you should always respect where photography is prohibited or restricted. By law, you should not make any footage from police or army objects.
In connection with people, you will find that the Egyptians love to take photos. But ethically, don't take pictures of (Egyptian) women without their permission.
Passport (Travel documents)
We often get the question from the tourists whether it is necessary to carry the passport/ID with you at all times when going to the tour.
Usually no. If we talk about the short day trips. Even if you travel to Cairo and Luxor on the tourist bus, you will not be asked for the documents as a foreigner. You are usually registered with your tour operator with your name and hotel, as the excursions require a permit from the Ministry of Tourism and the Tourist Police.
Take the passports or ID with you on air tours , hotel overnight tours and when traveling through the Sinai Peninsula.
Otherwise – on safari, sea trips and city tours you can keep the documents in the hotel safe. It's always useful to take the copy of the last/data page with you. However, what we do recommend is that you always carry your travel insurance card with you.
Do you have more questions or just need real honest advice? You can leave your comment at any time. We will get back to you. Because we love Egypt and would love to guide you around.

title: Egypt and COVID-19

sections.Egypt and COVID-19:
While the Europe is foreseeing the 3th or 4th wave of COVID-19, many of us are saying that we cannot stay without traveling anymore. Traveling to Egypt feels like a heaven and in some way of word- it is. Lots of natural vitamin D, sun, sea and fresh air. Sitting on the beach, no masks wearing, no respirators...what can be better? But what are the rules of vacationing in the Red Sea now?
First of all, be ready, that you will need to make PCR tests more than usual. Most probably, you will need to present the negative test while boarding in your destination. Make sure, you know all the rules of your aviation company. Many of them need to see your PCR test before flying you out. Your test, if you want to use it by arrival in Egypt , should not be older than 72 hours, some destinations have exceptional 96 hours. Please check this by purchasing the ticket. Egypt airports are requesting the PCR tests (incl. children from 6 years) , just in some destinations (Hurghada and Sharm inclusive) you are able to make PCR test upon arrival at the airport. The cost is 30 USD. The visa in Hurghada airport for charter flights is again paid from 1.5.2021, 25 USD.
After making test at the airport you suppose to isolate for 24 hours in your hotel room or apartment till receiving the result. If you tested negative, you will not get any message, if your result is positive, you will be contacted by testing team (as you are providing them with your actual phone number). In the case you need to change your travel plan, please be sure your travel insurance is covering COVID-19.
As we hope all of you will arrive safe and healthy, you can start to enjoy your sunny days without any problems. The hotels are following all the precautions and measures against spreading the virus, desinfecting places, distance rules and personal wearing face masks. We recommend you to wear the mask if you are going indoors, shopping, visiting pharmacy etc. You don’t need to wear it in the hotel. The hotel restaurants are serving the food, sitting tables are in distance, many items and tools are sterile packed.
If you wish to make the day trips – boat trip, sea trip , desert safari , quad driving, you are not in need to show your PCR tests. Sightseeing trips to Cairo , Luxor , Aswan , Abu Simbel are still working. For these drives you can use your PCR test, which you did in your country.
There are currently no restrictions which would stop your dream to enjoy the sun, beach and real vacation!
When it comes to the departure and returning home, again, check the rules of your air company. If they need you to present the negative PCR test again, there are plenty possibilities to make your test in Hurghada on time. You can choose the central lab in Dahar or call the private hospital, which can get your sample to the lab and provide you with the result stamped by Ministry of health. Results can take up to 36 hours, therefore be sure, you make the reservation before the flight in advance. If you are heading to governmentally shared central lab in Dahar Hurghada ( https://maps.app.goo.gl/MUTngY3jxS8kj1ue8 ) , prepare yourself with the passport copies, flight ticket or accommodation copy. Some countries and air companies are currently accepting also antigen test results, these you can obtain in many Hurghada laboratories.
Traveling in these turbulent times is not easy. Many restrictions, flight cancelations, quarantine, testing. We believe all of us should think about health, for ourselves but also for our families. But health is not only physical but very important mental. Social isolation can cause damages and traveling is one of the receipts which we wish could be prescribed by doctors. Hopefully soon we are able to see each other in our favorite holiday places in person.

title: Departure from Egypt and Home Flight

sections.Departure from Egypt and Home Flight:
As your stay in Egypt comes to an end, careful preparation can ensure your departure is as seamless as your arrival. While the country’s airports are well-equipped to handle high volumes of international travelers, a structured approach to documentation, luggage, and airport procedures is essential.
Documentation: Non-Negotiable Essentials Before heading to the airport, confirm that all travel documents are in order. This includes your passport (or national ID where applicable), entry visa, and arrival card. These documents are required for exit procedures.
In the event of a lost passport or ID, travelers should immediately contact their embassy to arrange emergency documentation. If the airport arrival card or visa record is missing, assistance from your airline representative or travel agent will be required before departure clearance can be granted.
It is also advisable to reconfirm your return flight with the airline or travel provider at least 24 hours in advance, as many carriers still require this step.
Baggage: Plan Ahead to Avoid Costs Excess luggage remains one of the most common departure challenges. Airlines operating from Egypt typically allow between 15–20 kg for checked baggage on charter flights, while low-cost carriers often apply stricter limits.
Travelers expecting to exceed these limits should pre-purchase additional baggage allowance directly through the airline. Paying at the airport is significantly more expensive and less predictable. After check-in, ensure that your baggage receipt is accurate—this is the only proof for tracking or claims if issues arise.
Security Procedures: Expect Multiple Checks Egyptian airports maintain rigorous security protocols, often involving several screening points before boarding. While this may extend processing time, it contributes to overall safety standards.
To minimize inconvenience, consolidate valuables into a single carry-on bag and avoid repeatedly unpacking items at each checkpoint. Be aware that certain items—such as power banks and USB lighters—may be restricted or subject to additional screening requirements.
Duty-Free and Customs: Know the Limits Purchases made at airport duty-free shops should comply with import regulations of your destination country. For example, within the European Union, travelers are generally permitted to carry up to 200 cigarettes per person. Exceeding these limits may result in confiscation or penalties upon arrival.
Customs authorities reserve the right to inspect luggage, so transparency and compliance are strongly recommended.
Health Considerations: Fit to Fly Requirements Travelers recovering from illness or injury should ensure they are medically cleared to fly. In certain cases, airport medical staff may deny boarding if a passenger is deemed unfit for travel.
Passengers carrying medication must present it during security screening, ideally in original packaging and accompanied by a doctor’s note if required. Special cases—such as recent surgery or casts—may require additional documentation, including a “Fit to Fly” certificate.
Airports also provide assistance services, including wheelchair support, which can be arranged in advance through your airline or tour operator.
Final Checks: Tickets, Timing, and Compliance Ensure that your ticket—whether digital or printed—matches your passport details exactly. Even minor discrepancies can delay check-in. Airport counters typically close one hour prior to departure, so early arrival is strongly advised.
It is important to note that most Red Sea airports, including Hurghada, Marsa Alam , and Sharm El Sheikh, primarily handle charter operations. Additionally, travelers staying longer than three months must use scheduled (non-charter) flights and ensure their visa status is valid to avoid penalties.
Contrary to some misconceptions, Egypt does not impose a departure tax at the airport.
Executive Takeaway A smooth departure from Egypt is largely a matter of preparation. Ensuring document accuracy, managing baggage proactively, and understanding airport procedures can significantly reduce friction. For travelers and operators alike, these steps reinforce efficiency and help maintain a positive end-to-end travel experience.
P.S .: If you have any further questions or would like to your experience regarding your departure, please let us know and do not hesitate to leave a comment!

title: Weather in Egypt

sections.Weather in Egypt:
Weather in Egypt: A Year-Round Sun Destination with Seasonal Nuance
Egypt’s climate is defined by its geography—positioned in North Africa and dominated by arid desert conditions. Along the Mediterranean coast and across the Nile Delta, the weather shifts to a milder, Mediterranean pattern, while the elevated Sinai Mountains can see notably cooler temperatures during winter months.
For most travelers, however, Egypt means sun. Red Sea destinations such as Hurghada, Marsa Alam, and Sharm El Sheikh rank among the warmest year-round locations, offering consistently dry conditions and minimal rainfall. While wind can occasionally be a factor, extended periods of cold or wet weather are rare—one of the country’s strongest tourism advantages.
Winter: Mild Days, Cooler Nights January marks the coolest period of the year. Daytime temperatures in coastal resort areas typically range between 20–23°C, making it comfortable for outdoor activities. Midday sun provides warmth for beachgoers, but mornings and evenings can feel brisk, often requiring a light jacket. Wind is more noticeable, and sea temperatures may deter more sensitive swimmers.
From February through April, conditions improve steadily. Temperatures rise to a pleasant 23–28°C, creating ideal circumstances for both relaxation and exploration. However, this period can bring occasional strong winds, particularly during the “Khamsin”—a seasonal phenomenon known for dry, sand-laden gusts that may develop over roughly 50 days.
Spring: The Optimal Balance May stands out as one of the most favorable months. With daytime temperatures ranging from 30–35°C, the climate strikes a balance between warmth and comfort. It’s especially suitable for families and travelers seeking to combine sightseeing with coastal leisure, as sea temperatures also become inviting.
Summer: Peak Heat, Peak Sun Between June and September, Egypt enters its hottest phase. Average temperatures range from 35–40°C, climbing even higher in southern regions. These months cater to travelers seeking guaranteed sunshine, particularly within all-inclusive resort settings. Proper precautions—hydration, sun protection, and shaded rest—are essential during this period.
Autumn: Prime Travel Season October is widely regarded as one of the best months to visit. The intense summer heat subsides, yet the sea remains warm and welcoming. This is an ideal time for broader travel experiences, including cultural visits to Cairo and Luxor , Nile cruises, desert excursions, and snorkeling in the Red Sea.
Late Autumn to Early Winter: Gradual Cooling November offers stable, moderate conditions, with temperatures easing back to around 25°C. By late November and into December, cooler patterns emerge. While still mild compared to many regions, evenings become noticeably cooler, particularly for travelers seeking consistent warmth during the holiday season.
Executive Takeaway Egypt’s climate is a strategic asset for its tourism sector—delivering near year-round sunshine with predictable seasonal variations. Peak comfort windows fall in spring and autumn, while summer appeals to heat-tolerant travelers and winter offers mild, quieter conditions. For both leisure and cultural tourism, Egypt remains a resilient, weather-reliable destination.
suggest 3 fotos
Here are three strong, publication-ready photo ideas that match the tone of the article and appeal to a CEO-level audience:
Red Sea Resort Aerial (Hurghada or Sharm El Sheikh) A high-quality aerial shot showing turquoise water, resort infrastructure, and beach activity. This visually reinforces Egypt as a premium, year-round sun destination and highlights tourism investment and scale.
Nile with Luxor Temples at Sunset A warm, golden-hour image of the Nile River with temples or historical landmarks in the background. This balances the “sun destination” narrative with Egypt’s cultural depth and positions it as more than just a beach market.
Desert Landscape with Khamsin Atmosphere A dramatic shot of sand dunes with visible wind movement or a light sandstorm effect. This adds contrast, illustrating the unique climate dynamics mentioned in the article and giving the piece visual storytelling depth.

title: Private Customizable Hurghada City Tour (5 Hours) with Hotel Pickup

sections.Private Customizable Hurghada City Tour:
5 hours
Easy
Minimum age: 4
See Hurghada beyond the beach on a private, customizable city tour.
This 5-hour tour is designed for travelers who want local life, waterfront views, and markets in one easy afternoon. Travel by air-conditioned vehicle with a professional guide (English, German, or French) and shape the route around what interests you most.
What you’ll do (customizable highlights)
Stroll the waterfront at Hurghada’s New Marina area
Explore local streets for shopping and everyday city life (Sheraton Road / city boulevards)
Visit traditional markets in the old town area (Dahar souq)
Photo stops at key landmarks such as the marina mosque area and the Coptic cathedral area, based on your pace and interests
Timing & pickup
Duration: 5 hours
Start time: 09:00 / 15:00
Meeting type: hotel pickup and drop-off in the Hurghada area
Good to know
Wear comfortable walking shoes for market streets and short walks
For mosque areas, bring modest clothing that covers shoulders and knees
Food, drinks, shopping, and any paid attractions are not included
Experience Hurghada at your pace — from marina views to old-town markets.
Hotel pickup and drop-off (Hurghada area)
Transportation by air-conditioned vehicle
Professional tour guide (English, German, or French)
Customizable city route including the listed Hurghada highlights
Bottled water during the tour
Food & drinks
Personal expenses and shopping
Gratuities
Entrance fees for Hurghada Museum or any other paid attraction
Pickup & Meeting: Hotel pickup in Hurghada with pre-arranged meeting details.
Duration: Daily departures at 09:00 AM and 03:00 PM. Total duration approx. 5 hours.
What to Bring: Comfortable shoes, water, and sun protection.
Included: Professional support and round-trip transfer within Hurghada.

title: Hurghada Dune Buggy Desert Safari with Hotel Pickup

sections.Hurghada Dune Buggy Desert Safari with H:
4 hours
Moderate
Minimum age: 18
Tour Overview
Enjoy an unforgettable Dune Buggy Tour in Hurghada and explore the spectacular landscapes of the Red Sea desert. This exciting desert safari takes you deep into the Eastern Desert where you will drive a powerful buggy across golden sand dunes, rocky valleys, and wide desert plains.
During the tour you will discover the natural beauty of the desert, stop for photos in breathtaking locations, and visit a traditional Bedouin camp. There you can relax with authentic Bedouin tea, experience local hospitality, and enjoy a short camel ride before continuing your desert adventure.
This Hurghada buggy safari is perfect for travelers looking for an action-packed activity combined with cultural experiences and stunning desert scenery.
Why Book This Tour
Drive your own dune buggy across the Hurghada desert
Enjoy a thrilling off-road desert safari adventure
Visit a traditional Bedouin village in the Eastern Desert
Experience authentic Bedouin tea and hospitality
Optional camel ride in the desert
Amazing photo opportunities with desert mountains and sand dunes
A fun and exciting activity suitable for couples, friends, and families
The Dune Buggy Safari Hurghada is one of the most popular desert excursions on the Red Sea because it combines adventure, nature, and traditional Bedouin culture in one unforgettable experience.
How the Experience Runs
Your desert adventure begins with pickup from your hotel in Hurghada and a comfortable transfer to the buggy station located in the desert.
After arrival, professional guides will give a safety briefing and explain how to operate the buggy. Once everyone is ready, the guided desert drive begins as you follow your guide across sandy trails and desert valleys.
During the tour you will stop at a Bedouin camp where you can relax, enjoy traditional Bedouin tea, and experience a short camel ride while learning about the Bedouin lifestyle.
After the break, you will continue your exciting buggy ride back through the desert to the starting point. At the end of the tour, transportation will be waiting to take you back to your hotel.
Tour Schedule
Hotel Pickup: 08:30 AM
Arrival at Buggy Station: 09:00 AM
Duration of the Experience: About 4 hours
Return to Hotel: Around 12:30 PM
Price:
Double Buggy (2 persons): 130 € total
65 € per person

title: Hula Hula Island Red Sea Snorkeling

sections.Hula Hula Island Red Sea Snorkeling:
8 hours
Easy
Enjoy a full-day Red Sea adventure to Hula Hula Island, one of the most beautiful snorkeling spots near Hurghada. This relaxing sea trip combines crystal-clear waters, vibrant coral reefs, and white sandy beaches with a comfortable boat experience.
The tour is ideal for all travelers—whether you want to snorkel among colorful marine life or simply relax on a stunning island. A buffet lunch, soft drinks, and professional guidance are included for a smooth and enjoyable day.
Morning pickup from your hotel in Hurghada
Transfer to the marina and boarding the boat
Sailing across the Red Sea with scenic views
First snorkeling stop at a coral reef with a guide
Arrival at Hula Hula Island and free beach time (approx. 1.5–2 hours)
Relax, swim, or enjoy the island atmosphere
Buffet lunch and soft drinks served on board
Optional water sports and second snorkeling stop
Afternoon return to the marina
Transfer back to your hotel
The full experience lasts around 7–8 hours depending on location and weather conditions.

title: Starwatching Desert Safari Experience in Hurghada

sections.Starwatching Desert Safari Experience in:
5 hours
Moderate
Minimum age: 4
Escape the city lights and experience an unforgettable evening under the stars with the Starwatching Desert Safari in Hurghada. This 5-hour adventure begins with a thrilling beach buggy ride and a short camel ride, followed by a cultural encounter with Bedouin women and traditional bread-making. As night falls, explore the desert sky using a high-precision GPS telescope, then unwind with a Bedouin dinner under the stars before returning to your hotel.
Clear, unobstructed desert skies perfect for stargazing.
Exciting mix of adventure and culture – buggy ride, camel ride, and Bedouin activities.
Professional stargazing equipment for an enhanced experience.
Breathtaking sunset and desert scenery.
Includes traditional Bedouin dinner in a relaxed desert camp setting.
Afternoon pick-up from Hurghada and drive into the desert.
Desert adventure: buggy ride through dunes, followed by a camel ride.
Bedouin cultural stop: witness bread-making and visit a trading post.
Sunset watching over the desert horizon.
Stargazing session: observe stars, constellations, and celestial objects.
Dinner and relaxation: enjoy Bedouin cuisine under the stars before returning to your hotel.
End your night with a magical desert stargazing adventure —an unforgettable blend of excitement, culture, and relaxation.
Infants must not sit on laps
Infant seats unavailable
Not recommended for people with back problems
Not recommended for pregnant travelers
Not recommended for people with heart conditions
Travellers are picked up from Hurghada hotels. Pick up in area of Sahl Hasheesh, El Gouna and Makadi is possible for additional 5 euro per person (to be paid upon pick up)

title: Private Full-Day Tour of Monasteries St Paul and St Antonius

sections.Private Full-Day Tour of Monasteries St:
9 hours
Moderate
Minimum age: 4
Discover Egypt’s spiritual heritage on a private full-day tour to the ancient desert monasteries of Saint Anthony and Saint Paul the Anchorite. Hidden in the dramatic mountains of the Eastern Desert near the Red Sea, these historic sites are among the world’s most important Christian pilgrimage destinations.
Founded in the 4th century, they mark the birthplace of Christian monastic life. On this journey from Hurghada, travel through stunning desert landscapes, explore centuries-old churches and fortifications, and learn about the early desert monks who shaped Christian history.
This private excursion blends culture, history, and spirituality, offering a peaceful experience far from the busy Red Sea resorts.
Visit the oldest working Christian monastery in the world, founded in honor of Anthony the Great , considered the father of Christian monasticism. Inside the monastery walls you will discover:
The historic Church of St. Anthony with its beautiful Coptic frescoes
The saint’s tomb and sacred religious sites
Ancient manuscripts and a historic monastic library
Peaceful courtyards surrounded by desert mountains
Continue to the nearby monastery dedicated to Paul of Thebes , believed to be the first Christian hermit. Built near the cave where he lived for decades in solitude, this monastery offers:
The cave where St. Paul spent much of his life
Ancient chapels and historic religious icons
Stories and traditions from early Christian desert life
Stunning views of the surrounding desert mountains
How the Day Works
1. Hotel Pickup & Desert Journey Your day begins with an early morning pickup from your hotel in Hurghada in a comfortable, air-conditioned private vehicle. Enjoy the scenic drive through the vast Eastern Desert with impressive mountain landscapes along the way.
2. Visit the Ancient Monasteries Upon arrival, your guide will accompany you through the Monastery of Saint Anthony and the Monastery of Saint Paul. Learn about their fascinating history, architecture, and spiritual significance while exploring the churches, courtyards, and peaceful surroundings.
3. Lunch Break & Return to Hurghada You may stop for an optional lunch at a local restaurant before beginning the afternoon journey back to Hurghada. Arrival at your hotel is expected in the late afternoon after a memorable cultural experience.

title: Luxor Day Trip from Hurghada by Minibus: Karnak, Valley of the Kings & Hatshepsut

sections.Luxor Day Trip from Hurghada by Minibus::
16 hours
Moderate
See Luxor’s most important ancient sites in one full-day trip from Hurghada, traveling by air-conditioned minibus with an Egyptologist guide.
At a glance • Duration: 16 hours • Pickup: hotel pick-up and drop-off in Hurghada (from your hotel entrance) • Included: entrance fees to Karnak Temple, the Valley of the Kings, and the Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, plus lunch and bottled water
After an early-morning pickup from your Hurghada hotel entrance, you’ll travel south toward Luxor, passing Upper Egyptian villages along the way. You’ll spend the day exploring the major highlights on both sides of the Nile with your guide, then begin the drive back toward Hurghada after the program finishes around 17:00 in Luxor.
Start at Karnak Temple, one of the world’s great temple complexes. Walk the huge entrance area, see the avenue of ram‑headed sphinxes, and step into the Hypostyle Hall with its towering columns. You’ll also see the sacred lake and surrounding chapels. Near the lake, your guide can point out the famous scarab that many visitors circle as a local legend.
After lunch, visit the Valley of the Kings and go down into three royal tombs to see the remarkably preserved wall paintings and hieroglyphs.
Stop for photos at the Colossi of Memnon, the two massive statues of Pharaoh Amenhotep III that once guarded a vast mortuary temple.
Finish at the Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, known for its grand terraces, colonnades, and detailed reliefs. Afterward, you’ll have an opportunity to stop at a local perfume and papyrus workshop for browsing and souvenirs.
The sightseeing program in Luxor finishes around 16:00, then you’ll start the drive back toward Hurghada.
Enjoy a private excursion exclusively with your family or friends. ★ 2 people – from €135 per person ★ 3 people – from €110 per person
This tour is also available from Marsa Alam for an additional €50 per person, which covers a private transfer to Safaga and back.

title: Dendera and Abydos Private Tour From Hurghada

sections.Dendera and Abydos Private Tour From Hur:
17 hours
Moderate
Minimum age: 4
Discover the wonders of Upper Egypt on a private full-day tour from Hurghada, visiting two of the country’s most remarkable temples — the Temple of Dendera in Qena and the Temple of Abydos in Sohag.
Your private Egyptologist guide (German/English; other languages available upon request) will bring ancient history to life, sharing fascinating stories of gods, pharaohs, and sacred rituals in a relaxed and personalized setting.
Built mainly during the Ptolemaic Period (54–20 BC) under Ptolemy XII Auletes, with later Roman additions by Cleopatra VII. Admire colorful reliefs, zodiac carvings, and grand halls filled with detailed hieroglyphics.
Optional extras include exploring the crypts and secret chambers or climbing to the roof for unique views and astronomical symbolism.
Located about 2.5 hours north of Luxor, this magnificent temple was built during the New Kingdom, 19th Dynasty by Seti I and completed by Ramesses II. Explore the famous king list, sacred chambers, royal chapels, and beautifully preserved ritual reliefs in a peaceful and spiritual atmosphere.

title: Cairo Overday Tour from Hurghada by Flight

sections.Cairo Overday Tour from Hurghada by Flig:
1 day
Moderate
Enjoy an unforgettable full-day trip from Hurghada to Cairo by domestic flight. Accompanied by a certified Egyptologist guide, you will explore Egypt’s most famous ancient landmarks while learning fascinating stories about the country’s rich history and civilization.
This tour combines comfort, fast travel, and world-famous attractions — all in one incredible day. Guests can choose between visiting the classic Egyptian Museum or upgrading to the spectacular Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM). A delicious lunch at a local restaurant is included during the tour.
Visit the last remaining Wonder of the Ancient World. Explore the Great Pyramid of Khufu, the Pyramid of Khafre, and the Pyramid of Menkaure while enjoying panoramic views of the Giza Plateau.
See the legendary Sphinx, the mysterious guardian of the pyramids with the body of a lion and the face of a pharaoh.
Egyptian Museum: Home to thousands of priceless artifacts, including the treasures of Tutankhamun.
Grand Egyptian Museum (Optional Upgrade): Discover the world’s newest and largest archaeological museum, featuring extraordinary ancient masterpieces displayed in a modern setting.
Enjoy a freshly prepared lunch at a carefully selected local restaurant during your sightseeing tour.

title: Neverland - Musical and Cabaret Show

sections.Neverland - Musical and Cabaret Show:
4 hours
Easy
Hurghada evening: music, dance, cabaret, dinner, drinks, private transfers.
Neverland Show – Musical and Cabaret Show is a vibrant international evening experience in Hurghada, combining music, dance, lights, and theatre in one spectacular performance.
The show takes place inside the impressive Neverland Palace theatre (Oriental Palace / Alf Leila Wa Leila venue) and blends traditional and modern entertainment in a magical setting.
Your experience includes dinner, a varied drinks selection, private round-trip transfers, and a special welcome surprise: upon arrival, you will receive a traditional Egyptian jellabiya (loose traditional outfit) to wear and fully enjoy the oriental atmosphere.
A spectacular music and cabaret show featuring international cabaret acts, pop dance performances, and traditional oriental music
Professional choreography, stunning costumes, and impressive light and stage effects
A festive and engaging atmosphere throughout the evening
A delicious multi-course dinner including Moroccan salads, chicken tagine with lemon and saffron sauce, grilled kebab and kofta with vegetables, French pastries, oriental cookies
Drinks included: soft drinks, lager beer, and local wines
Reservation must be made before 12:00 on the day of the show
Private transfer from your hotel starting around 18:00 (exact time depends on your hotel location)
Welcome jellabiya upon ent rance
Enjoy dinner, drinks, and the full live show
Private transfer back to your hotel after the performance

title: Quad Biking on the Coast of Makadi Bay

sections.Tour Overview:
Enjoy an exciting quad bike adventure through the desert of Makadi Bay and along the beautiful Red Sea coastline. This 3.5-hour experience combines off-road riding, sea views, sand dunes, and a short camel ride for the perfect mix of adrenaline and local atmosphere.
Double quads are available for two persons on one bike. The minimum driving age is 16 years.
Pickup time is approximately 8:00 AM from Hurghada hotels and 8:30 AM from Makadi Bay hotels. Hotel transfers and refreshments are included for a comfortable and hassle-free trip.
Unique combination of desert and sea landscapes in one tour
Ride along the Red Sea coastline — a rare quad biking experience
Double quad option available for couples or friends
Beginner-friendly with full safety briefing and guidance
Includes camel ride and Bedouin tea experience
Convenient morning pickup from Hurghada and Makadi Bay
Perfect for couples, friends, and families looking for adventure beyond the resort
Hotel Pickup & Safety Briefing Morning pickup from your hotel in Hurghada or Makadi Bay, followed by welcome tea, safety instructions, and a short driving test.
Desert & Coastal Adventure Quad biking across open desert and sand dunes, with a ride toward the Red Sea coast for scenic views and photos.
Camel Ride & Return Transfer Enjoy a short traditional camel experience at the station, then transfer back to your hotel.
This tour is ideal for couples, friends, and families looking for an active and memorable experience outside the hotel resort.

title: Hurghada Dolphin House Snorkeling Boat Trip with Hotel Pickup (8 Hours)

sections.Hurghada Dolphin House Snorkeling Boat T:
8 hours
Easy
Spend a full day on the Red Sea from Hurghada with hotel pickup and an 8-hour boat trip focused on snorkeling and dolphin observation in their natural s urroundings. You’ll sail from the Hurghada marina/port toward the Dolphin House area, snorkel over colorful coral reefs, and enjoy lunch on board.
• Hotel pickup is around 8:00 a.m., then transfer by bus to the Hurghada port.
• Pick up your snorkeling equipment at the port (included), then board the boat.
• First stop: the Dolphin House area / house reef for snorkeling and dolphin watching.
• Lunch on board with a soft drink.
• Second stop: another coral reef site for more snorkeling.
• Extra fun on the water: banana boat and couch boat rides.
After the cruise, the boat returns to the port and you’ll be transferred back to your hotel.
Hurghada transfers are included. For hotels outside Hurghada there is a transfer surcharge: El Gouna, Makadi Bay, Sahl Hasheesh +€5 per person; Soma Bay and Safaga +€10 per person.

title: El Gouna City Tour and Lagoon Trip

sections.El Gouna City Tour and Lagoon Trip:
4 hours
Easy
Discover the beautiful resort town of El Gouna, often called the “Venice of the Red Sea.” Built around scenic lagoons and canals, El Gouna is known for its elegant architecture, marina, and relaxed atmosphere.
The tour begins with a stop at a viewing platform overlooking El Gouna, offering panoramic views of the town, golf courses, and lagoons. Next, enjoy a 1-hour lagoon boat trip through the canals, where you can admire the villas, hotels, and bridges that make El Gouna unique.
After the boat trip, enjoy a traditional Egyptian lunch at a restaurant in El Gouna, including ful, freshly fried falafel, local baladi bread, and a refreshing soft drink. The tour continues to explore the town with a professional guide available in English, German, or French.

title: Parasailing in Hurghada – Fly Above the Red Sea!

sections.Parasailing in Hurghada – Fly Above the:
15 minutes
Easy
Parasailing over the Red Sea is an unforgettable adventure in Hurghada, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the coastline and crystal‑clear waters. Whether you’re traveling solo or with a friend, this activity lets you soar up to 500 feet high in a smooth, safe flight with no prior experience needed. You’ll launch from a platform on the back of a speedboat, enjoy about 10–13 minutes in the air, and take in the fresh sea breeze and stunning scenery. The experience is suitable for families, couples, and solo travelers alike, with daily departures and affordable prices.
sections.What You Will Do:
Meet your friendly and experienced crew who will handle all safety and equipment.
Launch from a specially designed platform attached to a speedboat.
Parasail up to 500 feet above the Red Sea, taking in panoramic coastal views.
Enjoy a 10–13 minute flight above the water with smooth takeoff and landing.
Relax and take memorable photos from a unique perspective above Hurghada’s shoreline.
sections.Pickup Area & Transfer Surcharges:
Included: Transfer from hotels within Hurghada.
Surcharges: Transfer from hotels outside Hurghada is available for an additional 5 EUR per person.
sections.Availability: The tour is offered daily, giving you flexibility to choose the best day during your stay in Hurghada.
sections.Price Options:

Double Parasailing (2 people): €30 per pair

title: Day on the Ozirea Island

sections.Day on the Ozirea Island:
8 hours
Easy
Enjoy a relaxing day on Ozirea Island , one of the newest island experiences near Hurghada. Surrounded by clear turquoise water and colorful coral reefs, it’s the perfect place to swim, snorkel, and unwind in the Red Sea.
This full-day boat trip includes snorkeling stops at vibrant reefs, free time on the island’s sandy beach, and a buffet lunch with drinks. It’s an ideal excursion for couples, families, and anyone looking to enjoy the beauty of the Red Sea.
Your day starts with hotel pickup in Hurghada and transfer to the marina. From there, you will sail across the Red Sea toward Ozirea Island.
Along the way, enjoy a snorkeling stop to explore coral reefs and tropical fish. After arriving at the island, you will have free time to relax on the beach or swim in the clear water. Lunch with drinks is served during the trip before sailing back to the marina and transferring to your hotel in the afternoon.

title: Horse Riding (Desert and the Beach) in Hurghada

sections.Horse Riding (Desert and the Beach) in H:
3 hours
Easy
Experience one of the most beautiful outdoor adventures in Hurghada with an unforgettable horseback ride through the desert and along the Red Sea coast.
Discover the region’s natural beauty on well-trained Arabian horses, guided by professional instructors. The combination of golden desert landscapes and the turquoise waters of the Red Sea creates a truly unique experience—perfect for nature lovers and adventure seekers.
Choose between an early morning ride under the bright sun or a romantic sunset ride, both offering breathtaking views and excellent photo opportunities.
Your adventure begins with hotel pickup in Hurghada and transfer to the horse ranch.
Upon arrival, professional trainers will introduce you to your horse and provide a short safety briefing along with basic riding instructions—making this tour suitable for beginners as well.
Once ready, you will start riding through the peaceful and open desert landscape, enjoying the calm atmosphere and stunning surroundings.
The journey continues toward the Red Sea coast, where you will ride along the beautiful shoreline, feel the fresh sea breeze, and enjoy spectacular views.
There are plenty of opportunities to take memorable photos during the ride—both in the desert and along the beach.
After the ride, you will return to the stable before being transferred back to your hotel.

5 hours
Moderate
Pack the highlights of a Hurghada desert safari into one plan: ride a quad bike, enjoy a speed buggy drive, take a camel ride, visit a Bedouin village, and finish with a BBQ dinner and folklore show. Hotel pickup and drop-off by Jeep are included, so you can focus on the experience.
All-in-one safari: quad bike + buggy + camel ride, plus a Bedouin village visit.
Evening finish included: BBQ dinner under the open sky with folklore entertainment.

2) Quad bike ride After a safety briefing and helmet fitting, head out on your quad bike ride across the desert.
3) Bedouin village visit Stop at a Bedouin village to experience local hospitality, including Bedouin tea and shisha.
4) Speed buggy drive and camel ride Enjoy your speed buggy drive and a camel ride as part of the safari program.
5) BBQ dinner and folklore show End the experience with BBQ dinner and a folklore show before your Jeep transfer back to your hotel.

title: Cairo & Giza Day Trip from Hurghada (Small Group) – Pyramids, Sphinx & Egyptian Museum

sections.Cairo & Giza Day Trip from Hurghada (Sma:
19 hours
Moderate
Cairo in one full day from Hurghada This small-group day trip is built for travelers who want to see Cairo’s essential highlights in a single, well-organized outing. You’ll travel in an air-conditioned vehicle with a professional Egyptologist guide and visit the Giza Plateau (Pyramids and Sphinx) plus the Egyptian Museum in Tahrir Square.
The Giza Plateau Stand in front of the Great Pyramids of Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure and see the Great Sphinx at the edge of the ancient necropolis.
Ancient Discoveries See remarkable historical finds in Cairo, including a 4,600-year-old cedarwood vessel associated with Pharaoh Khufu.
The Egyptian Museum (Tahrir Square) Explore one of Egypt’s most important museum collections, with highlights that include the mummies of Yuya and Tuya.
Guiding and pace Your licensed Egyptologist shares context and stories throughout the day. This is a long sightseeing day (total duration: 19 hours), best for travelers comfortable with a full schedule.
Pickup and transport Hotel pickup and drop-off are included from Hurghada. Pickup is also offered from El Gouna and Makadi, with an additional charge for some areas (see “Important traveler notes”).
Start time and duration Total experience duration is 19 hours. Your exact pickup time is provided with your booking details.
What’s included Entrance fees to the Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx area, and the Egyptian Museum are included, along with bottled water.

title: Diving (Intro) Hurghada

sections.Diving (Intro) Hurghada:
8 hours
Moderate
Minimum age: 6
Discover the incredible underwater world of the Red Sea with an exciting introductory scuba diving experience in Hurghada. This full-day boat trip is perfect for beginners who want to try scuba diving for the first time under the supervision of professional instructors.
Explore vibrant coral reefs, swim alongside colorful tropical fish, and experience the beauty of one of the world’s most famous diving destinations. No previous diving experience is required, and all diving equipment is included. Between dives, relax on the boat, enjoy lunch, and take in the stunning sea views.
Your experience begins with hotel pickup in Hurghada and transfer to the marina where the diving boat departs. After a short safety briefing from professional instructors, the boat sails to a beautiful coral reef in the Red Sea for your first introductory dive.
After the dive, relax on board and enjoy lunch while the boat moves to another reef site. Later, you will have the chance for a second guided dive or simply snorkel and swim.
In the afternoon, the boat returns to the marina before the transfer back to your hotel in Hurghada.
Free pickup and drop-off from hotels in Hurghada
Makadi Bay / El Gouna / Sahl Hasheesh: €5 per person
Safaga: €10 per person
Pickup is arranged directly from your hotel entrance or a nearby meeting point.
Available: Daily
Start time: around 08:00 AM
Return time: late afternoon to your hotel in Hurghada
Whether you are trying scuba diving for the first time or simply want to explore the beauty of the Red Sea, this trip offers an unforgettable adventure during your stay in Hurghada.

title: Tour to Abu Dabbab from Hurghada

sections.Tour to Abu Dabbab from Hurghada:
14 hours
Moderate
The Abu Dabbab Tour from Hurghada is one of the best snorkeling trips in the Red Sea. Abu Dabbab is a unique and beautiful beach located in Marsa Alam, Egypt, known for its calm, shallow waters and rich marine life.
On this full-day trip, you will travel along the scenic Red Sea coast to Abu Dabbab Bay, a protected natural area famous for its green sea turtles and occasional dugong sightings. The bay’s seagrass meadows attract sea turtles and other marine species, while nearby coral reefs are home to colorful tropical fish and vibrant coral formations.
Enjoy guided snorkeling in crystal-clear waters, relax on the sandy beach, and spend the day exploring one of Egypt’s most fascinating marine environments.
Snorkel in the crystal-clear waters of Abu Dabbab Bay, one of the best snorkeling locations in the Red Sea.
Swim above natural seagrass meadows where sea turtles are often seen feeding.
Discover colorful coral reefs and a wide variety of tropical reef fish.
Enjoy two guided snorkeling sessions with experienced staff.
Relax on the beautiful sandy beach and enjoy the stunning Red Sea scenery.
Have a beachside lunch with drinks included at a local restaurant.
1) Early Morning Hotel Pickup – 6:00 AM Your day begins with pickup from your hotel in Hurghada at around 6:00 AM. Travel in a comfortable air-conditioned vehicle along the Red Sea coast toward Abu Dabbab Bay.
2) Arrival & Snorkeling Briefing Upon arrival, your guide will provide a short safety briefing and help you prepare your snorkeling equipment before entering the water.
3) First Snorkeling Experience Enjoy your first snorkeling session in the clear waters of Abu Dabbab, where you can swim above seagrass meadows and search for sea turtles, colorful fish, and other marine life.
4) Lunch Break & Beach Relaxation After snorkeling, enjoy lunch at a beach restaurant with drinks included, followed by free time to relax on the sandy beach or swim.
5) Second Snorkeling Session & Return to Hurghada After another snorkeling session, you will leave Abu Dabbab at around 2:00 PM and travel back to Hurghada, arriving at your hotel at approximately 4:00 – 5:00 PM.
Tour Days: Monday & Friday
This unforgettable trip to Abu Dabbab offers the perfect mix of adventure and relaxation, with the unique opportunity to swim alongside the Red Sea’s famous sea turtles in one of Egypt’s most beautiful natural bays.

title: Sharm El Naga Snorkeling Trip from Hurghada – Coral Reef & Beach Relaxation

sections.Sharm El Naga Snorkeling Trip from Hurgh:
8 hours
Easy
Escape the crowds and discover the hidden beauty of Sharm El Naga, one of the most stunning natural bays on the Red Sea coast near Hurghada. Located about 40 km south of the city close to Safaga, this protected nature reserve is famous for its crystal-clear water, colorful coral reefs, and peaceful sandy beach.
Sharm El Naga is one of the best snorkeling destinations in the region. The reef begins just a few steps from the shore, making it perfect for beginners, families with children, and anyone who prefers easy access to the water. While snorkeling, you will discover vibrant coral formations, tropical fish, and sometimes even sea turtles.
For those who prefer to relax, the beautiful sandy beach offers comfortable sun loungers, umbrellas, showers, and stunning views of the Red Sea. Spend the day swimming, snorkeling, or simply enjoying the tranquil atmosphere of this natural paradise. A delicious lunch at the beach restaurant is included, making your day both relaxing and unforgettable.
1) Hotel Pickup Your adventure begins with a comfortable morning pickup from your hotel in Hurghada, Makadi Bay, or Soma Bay. Travel in an air-conditioned vehicle along the scenic Red Sea coast toward Sharm El Naga.
2) Arrival & Snorkeling Preparation After arriving at the nature reserve, you will receive snorkeling equipment and a short orientation before entering the water.
3) Snorkeling & Beach Relaxation Step directly from the sandy beach into the warm, clear water and explore one of the Red Sea’s most accessible coral reefs. Swim among colorful fish, admire the corals, and enjoy plenty of free time to relax on the beach.
4) Lunch by the Sea Enjoy a freshly prepared lunch at the beach restaurant with beautiful sea views.
5) Return to the Hotel In the late afternoon, relax during the drive back to your hotel in Hurghada.
Hotel pickup is available from Hurghada, Makadi Bay, and Soma Bay. The journey to Sharm El Naga takes approximately 45–60 minutes in a modern air-conditioned vehicle.
Whether you want to snorkel colorful coral reefs, swim in crystal-clear waters, or simply relax on a beautiful sandy beach, the Sharm El Naga Snorkeling Tour from Hurghada promises a perfect day of nature, sunshine, and unforgettable Red Sea scenery

title: Voyage Bleu (Blue Trip)

sections.Voyage Bleu (Blue Trip):
7 hours
Easy
Discover the beauty of the Red Sea on this unforgettable full-day Voyage Bleu snorkeling trip in Hurghada. Combining relaxation, comfort, and adventure, this small-group boat excursion offers crystal-clear waters, vibrant coral reefs, and a welcoming professional crew—perfect for a memorable day at sea.
Tour Overview Your day starts with hotel pickup at 8:00 AM, followed by a comfortable transfer to the marina. At 9:00 AM, you’ll set sail across the stunning Red Sea.
Guided Snorkeling Experience Enjoy two carefully selected snorkeling stops: ● First stop – 10:00 AM: Swim among tropical fish and coral reefs in crystal-clear water. ● Second stop – 1:45 PM: Continue exploring the underwater world with another guided session.

This tour is suitable for both beginners and experienced snorkelers.

You can also enjoy free fishing time with equipment provided.
Return Transfer At 4:00 PM, return to the marina followed by hotel transfer.

title: Cairo Day Trip from Hurghada – Pyramids & Grand Egyptian Museum

sections.Cairo Day Trip from Hurghada – Pyramids:
18 hours
Moderate
The Cairo Day Trip from Hurghada – Pyramids & Grand Egyptian Museum is a full-day guided excursion that lets you explore Egypt’s most iconic ancient sites in a single, well-planned itinerary. Starting early from Hurghada, you’ll travel in an air-conditioned vehicle to Cairo to visit the Giza Pyramids, the Great Sphinx, and the world-class Grand Egyptian Museum, with lunch included. The day concludes with a comfortable return to your hotel in the evening.
Giza Pyramids – Explore the legendary tombs of the pharaohs: the Great Pyramid of Cheops, Pyramid of Chephren, and Pyramid of Mykerinos.
Great Sphinx – Stand before the iconic lion-bodied guardian of the Giza Plateau.
Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) – Discover thousands of priceless ancient Egyptian artifacts, including the complete treasures of Tutankhamun in a modern museum setting.
Lunch – Enjoy a delicious midday meal at a local restaurant to recharge before continuing your cultural journey.
Early Morning Pickup – Depart from your Hurghada hotel at approximately 1:30 AM in an air-conditioned vehicle.
Arrival at Giza Plateau – Begin your guided visit to the pyramids and the Great Sphinx during the morning.
Lunch Break – Around 12:00 PM, enjoy a meal at a local restaurant.
Grand Egyptian Museum Visit – From roughly 1:30 PM to 3:00 PM, explore the museum’s extensive collection.
Return Journey – Travel back to Hurghada with an expected arrival between 9:00 PM and 10:00 PM.
Start Time: ~1:30 AM pickup from your Hurghada hotel
Total Duration: ~18 hours (including travel)
Return: Between 9:00 PM – 10:00 PM the same day
Experience the wonders of ancient Egypt in one unforgettable day with this expertly guided Cairo tour from Hurghada, including the Giza Pyramids, the Great Sphinx, and the Grand Egyptian Museum.

title: Hurghada Professional Diving Trip – Certified Divers Only

sections.Hurghada Professional Diving Trip – Cert:
8 hours
Moderate
Join a full-day professional diving trip in the Red Sea designed exclusively for certified divers (PADI, SSI, CMAS or equivalent). Explore two different dive sites around Hurghada’s famous coral reefs and enjoy guided dives with experienced dive masters. The trip includes boat cruising, relaxing surface intervals, lunch on board, and the opportunity to discover vibrant marine life and stunning underwater landscapes.
Daily diving trips usually include two dives at different locations with professional guidance, each lasting around 40–60 minutes depending on air consumption and certification level.
08:00 – Hotel Pickup
Pickup from your hotel in Hurghada and transfer to the marina.
08:30 – Arrival at Marina & Boat Boarding
Meet the diving team, check your certification card and logbook, prepare equipment, and board the dive boat.
09:00 – Boat Departure
Sail to the first dive site in the Red Sea while enjoying a briefing about the dive plan, safety procedures, and marine life.
10:00 – First Dive
Guided dive at the first reef site. Depth and dive profile depend on your certification level and experience.
11:30 – Surface Interval
Relax on the boat with drinks, sun deck time, or optional snorkeling while the boat moves to the second dive site.
12:30 – Lunch on Board
Fresh buffet lunch served with soft drinks, tea, coffee, and water.
14:00 – Second Dive
Second guided dive at a different reef or wall dive site with diverse marine life.
15:30 – Relax & Return Sailing
Enjoy the cruise back to the marina with time to relax and review your dive.
16:30 – Transfer to Hotel
Arrival at the marina and transfer back to your hotel.

title: Luxurious Beach of Mahmya

sections.Luxurious Beach of Mahmya:
8 hours
Easy
Minimum age: 4
Enjoy one of the most beautiful Red Sea island excursions with a full-day trip to Mahmya Island from Hurghada . Located inside the protected Giftun Island National Park , Mahmya Island is famous for its crystal-clear turquoise waters, soft white sandy beach, and vibrant coral reefs.
This relaxing Hurghada island tour is perfect for travelers looking to combine swimming, snorkeling, and beach relaxation in a stunning natural environment. Spend the day enjoying the breathtaking scenery of the Red Sea, snorkeling among colorful tropical fish, or simply relaxing on one of the most beautiful beaches near Hurghada. A delicious lunch and comfortable boat transportation make this excursion one of the most popular day trips in the region.
Transfer from your hotel to the marina in Hurghada.
Scenic boat cruise across the Red Sea to Mahmya Island.
Free time to relax, swim, or snorkel among coral reefs.
Enjoy a delicious lunch at the island’s beachfront restaurant.
Afternoon return by boat and transfer back to your hotel.
Hotel pickup is available from most hotels in Hurghada . A comfortable air-conditioned vehicle will transfer you from your hotel to the marina, where you will board the boat for your Mahmya Island excursion.
After the trip, you will return by boat to the marina and then be transferred back to your hotel. Pickup from nearby areas such as Makadi Bay , Sahl Hasheesh , and El Gouna may be available with an additional transfer fee.
A day trip to Mahmya Island is one of the best things to do in Hurghada, offering the perfect combination of Red Sea snorkeling, island relaxation, and unforgettable natural beauty .

sections.Private El Gouna City Tour & Lagoon Boat:
6 hours
Easy
Discover the charm of El Gouna, a beautiful resort town located about 25 km north of Hurghada. Often called the “Venice of Egypt,” El Gouna is famous for its picturesque lagoons, elegant marinas, and relaxed coastal atmosphere.
This guided day trip offers a relaxing escape from Hurghada, allowing you to explore the unique architecture, peaceful waterways, and stylish promenades of this modern Red Sea destination. During the excursion, you will enjoy a scenic boat ride across the lagoons, visit the marina area, and experience the distinctive lifestyle of this charming seaside town.
During your visit to El Gouna, you will explore several highlights of the town:
When you arrive in El Gouna, you will join a guided city tour and have a chance to look over the city from scenic viewpoints.
The beautiful lagoons and canals that connect the islands of the city
A relaxing felucca sailing experience on the calm waters
The elegant marina area with yachts, cafes, and waterfront promenades
Local markets and boutiques where you can browse for souvenirs
Modern architecture and scenic viewpoints across the resort town
El Gouna is known for its network of islands surrounded by lagoons and connected by bridges, giving it the nickname “Egyptian Venice.”

sections.Ocean Diva Catamaran:
5 hours
Easy
Enjoy a premium sailing experience in Hurghada aboard the Ocean Diva Catamaran , one of the most comfortable ways to explore the Red Sea. This relaxing sea excursion combines smooth sailing, snorkeling at stunning coral reefs, and high-quality onboard service.
The modern catamaran features spacious decks, shaded seating areas, and relaxing front nets where you can lie above the water and enjoy breathtaking panoramic views. During the trip, you will visit two snorkeling spots known for their vibrant coral reefs and diverse marine life.

title: Luxor Overday - Special Program

sections.Luxor Overday - Special Program:
1 day
Moderate
Visit the most beautiful places in Egypt. On this this excursion you will be welcomed at Luxor Temple, Valley of the Queens or in the most preserved temple of Medinat Habu.
Whoever didn't see Luxor was never really in Egypt! We believe that and if you have already visited the city and have not had enough, we offer something especially for you.
Luxor excursion - special
Pick up from the hotel is between 5 a.m. and 6 a.m. (Hurghada). As always, please order a breakfast package from reception the day before. Bring water and drinks, comfortable clothing and a good mood.
After 280 km drive with air-conditioned bus you will reach Luxor. The first stop on the West Bank is the Valley of the Queens, where all the important women of ancient Egypt rest in peace. No other grave here as valued as that of Nefertari, wife of Ramsess II / this grave is not included, daily entrances are also limited /
The next sightseeing stop is at the Medinat Habu Temple, which has stood here for 3,200 years and presents the power and battle success of Pharaoh Ramses III. Then a short visit to Memnon Colossi and then to the other bank of the Nile!
After lunch, Luxor Temple, which was connected to the Karnak Temple at the time, is waiting for you. This temple is special as you will find within ancient chapels, mosque and church!
Before 18:00 it is time to leave the city to make your way to Hurghada.
* Transfer surcharge 5 euro pp from hotels in Sahl Hasheesh and El Gouna.
Included:
Tickets (without Nefertari tomb)
Having lunch
German / English speaking guide
transfer
Not included:
Soft drink
Program extras
