title: Dolphin tour from Marsa Alam and El Quseir - Sataya

sections.Dolphin tour from Marsa Alam and El Quse:
1 day
Easy
Who doesn't want to experience it for themselves - dolphins in nature, swimming and playing! The definitely most popular trip in the Red Sea region is now also offered from Marsa Alam and El Quiser Hotels. Enjoy the incredible adventure of snorkeling in the most vibrant sea in the world. All you need is snorkeling gear (in most cases you can borrow it for free), towel, and sunscreen. Be on board and be part of nature.
We don't touch and hunt this wonderful creatures. We love them and try to be peaceful and calm with them
The areas of Marsa Alam and Hurghada are among the most beautiful in Egypt. Let yourself be taken there for a day.
The coral reefs show you more than you expected. There are complete coral gardens in the sea.
Don't touch and break the corals, don't collect shells and protect the Red Sea like we do. We show you beautiful places and ensure that you feel comfortable during the trip.
Pick-up time from Marsa Alam hotels is very early - between 5 and 6 in the morning. The return trip from the port to the hotel then in the late afternoon.
The drive to Hamata port takes around 1,5 hour. After this you are ready to onboard for sailing tour. During the boat drive we bring you to famous dolphin and mammals location Sataya. Snorkeling there is a must adventure!
The lunch will be on the boat, so get ready for another snorkeling round.
Time is enough to look around before you sail back to the port and are driven back to your hotel.
Excursion days:
Every day
Included:
Transfer from the hotel and back
snorkeling equipment
Lunch and soft drinks on board
English / German speaking guide
Please note that there is a new state tax "Protection Tax for the Red Sea National Park" that applies to all sea voyages. 3 euros p.p. will be picked up at the port.

title: Cairo Flight from Sharm El Sheikh

sections.Cairo Flight from Sharm El Sheikh:
1 day
Moderate
Discover the highlights of Egypt’s capital in just one day with a convenient flight from Sharm El Sheikh to Cairo. This full-day experience allows you to explore some of the world’s most iconic ancient sites, including the Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx, and the Egyptian Museum.
The tour includes return domestic flights, hotel transfers, a professional guide, entrance fees, and lunch—making it a comfortable and time-efficient way to experience Cairo without long travel hours.
Please note: It is highly recommended to confirm your booking at least one week in advance to ensure flight tickets are secured and availability is guaranteed.
Your day begins early with a hotel pickup and transfer to Sharm El Sheikh Airport for your flight to Cairo. Upon arrival, you’ll meet your guide and start exploring:
Giza Pyramids Complex Visit the legendary pyramids of Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure, along with the iconic Sphinx. Learn about ancient Egyptian burial traditions and the significance of these monuments.
Egyptian Museum Continue to Cairo’s famous museum, home to an extensive collection of artifacts, including statues, jewelry, mummies, and treasures from ancient Egypt.
Papyrus & Perfume Experience Stop at local workshops to discover traditional papyrus-making techniques and the secrets of Egyptian perfumes.
Lunch in Cairo Enjoy a meal at a local restaurant before continuing your exploration.
After a full day of sightseeing, you’ll head back to Cairo Airport for your return flight to Sharm El Sheikh, arriving at your hotel in the evening.
Inbegriffen:
Transport in modern and air-conditioned vehicles
National flights Sharm - Cairo and back
German / English speaking guide
Entrance fees to the above attractions
Lunch in Cairo
Not included:
Personal expenses for food, drinks or services that are not mentioned above
Entry to the Royal Mummy Room in the Egyptian Museum
Felucca ride on the Nile
Camel ride in the pyramids
Entry to the Great or Small Pyramid
Visit to the Solar Boot Museum
Useful tips:
The day before your trip, order the wake-up call and breakfast package at the reception of your hotel.
Please don't forget to take your passport with a visa.

title: Private Fishing Trip (Overday)

sections.Private Fishing Trip (Overday):
6 hours
Easy
Enjoy a private fishing adventure on the Red Sea, ideal for families, friends, or small groups seeking a relaxing yet exciting day on the water. With its rich marine life and favorable weather conditions, the Red Sea provides excellent fishing opportunities all year round.
Your day begins with a morning hotel pick-up, followed by a comfortable transfer to the port where your private boat awaits. Once onboard, you’ll sail to 2–3 carefully selected fishing spots, each offering a unique experience and a variety of fish species.
During the trip, you can fish, snorkel, or simply unwind on deck while taking in the stunning sea views. A freshly prepared lunch will be served on board between stops, and you’ll even have the option to have your catch cooked for you.
At the end of the day, you’ll return to the port and be transferred back to your hotel. This private tour guarantees a personalized and comfortable experience (minimum 2 participants required).

title: Orange Bay Island

sections.Orange Bay Island:
7 hours
Easy
Escape to paradise with a full-day excursion to Orange Bay Island, one of the most beautiful spots on the Red Sea, often called the “Caribbean of Egypt.” This experience perfectly combines relaxation and adventure, featuring crystal-clear turquoise waters, soft white sand, and vibrant marine life.
Your journey begins in the morning with a boat trip from the marina, sailing for around 40 minutes across the Red Sea to reach the island. Upon arrival, you’ll have free time to relax on the beach, swim in shallow lagoons, and enjoy the breathtaking scenery.
After your island visit, lunch is served onboard the boat. The trip then continues with two snorkeling stops at different coral reefs, where you can explore colorful fish and fascinating underwater life. Safety equipment is provided, making the activity suitable for beginners as well.
Later in the day, enjoy fun water activities such as banana boat and sofa rides before returning to the marina in the afternoon, ending a truly memorable day at sea.

Relax on white sandy beaches and swim in clear, shallow waters
Enjoy two snorkeling stops at vibrant coral reefs

Take in a scenic boat trip across the Red Sea

title: Jeep Desert Safari

sections.Jeep Desert Safari:
5 hours
Moderate
Jeep desert safari excursion - Marsa Alam
In this great excursion awaits you: jeep ride, camel ride, mountains, sand dune, tea with Bedouins, shisha / water pipe /, barbeque dinner
Fatamorgana and sand dune
You will be picked up from the hotel by jeep at around 1 p.m. You are driven about 45 minutes through the desert, where you reach Bedouin village. You would take a break near Elshaib Mountain, the highest mountain in the Arabian Desert. There you can see a Fatamorgana and be able to take pictures of the sand dune.
Bedouin
In the Bedouin village is waiting you camel ride, tea and shisha. Our Safari Guide gives you an idea of ​​the life of the Bedouin.
The women bring the water from the well, milk the camels, bake the bread, weave the carpets. That is why they also play a major role in desert life. They also drive pharmacies with natural products. The products naturally come from the desert.
There is always an opportunity for tourists to buy something. For example, there are products to whiten teeth. Natural deodorant is also very popular ....
Then let yourself be pampered with desert sunset. Bedouins invite you to dinner and a folklore program under the stars.
Included:
Jeep ride
Safari guide
barbeque dinner, soft drinks, shisha, camel riding
Our tips:
Bring a headscarf, good shoes, sunglasses, comfortable clothes / in the winter the evening desert is cold /

title: Diving Course

sections.Diving Course:
4 days
Moderate
Minimum age: 10
What is diving for beginners? If diving, then in Hurghada
You don't have much time but still want to do your first dives? Would you like to try whether diving is for you without investing too much time, money and energy? Are you worried if the Open Water Diver course is too difficult for you?
Then our diving course for beginners is perfect for you! It takes less time than the Open Water Diver course with less lessons and less training sessions in the swimming pool and in the sea.
Diving for beginners
Your certification is limited, but you can quickly get on the Open Water Diver course, and you can do some dives and dive to a depth of 10 meters, although you need to stay with a guide or instructor. You can also get air fillings, rent or buy diving equipment, and take other courses.
Theory and practice
You will learn the most important basics, theoretical knowledge and the skills of the diver during your sessions in the pool and on the open water.
You will learn about exercise skills such as buoyancy skills (comfortable ascents and descents, breathing under water), keeping warm under water. Streamline your swimming skills and buddy diving techniques. You collect information about diving environments and the aquatic life you want to learn about. For example, how to prevent common problems that can occur under water.
You will be introduced to boat diving. Of course, you get an impression of what it feels like to explore the underwater world.
After completing the course, you will receive an international certificate.
Please note:
You must be over 10 years old
You need to be physically fit and have no heart problems
Course content:
Daily theory and practice courses
5 theory and practice units in the pool / limited open water
at least 4 dives in the sea
Price:
20% discount for children under 11 years
For the pick-up service from hotels outside of Hurghada (i.e. in Makadi Bay, El Gouna, etc.) an additional transportation fee will be added.
Daily transfer from your hotel and back
All necessary equipment throughout the course
Lunch + a soft drink during the boat trip
Cost of materials and certificates
Additional drinks on board

title: Jeep safari Trip to Coloured Canyon (Sharm El Sheikh)

sections.Jeep safari Trip to Coloured Canyon (Sha:
11 hours
Moderate
Jeep Safari Excursion Colored Canyon is an unforgettable excursion. Experience the impressive rock formations of the Colored Canyon, visit Nuweiba and Dahab.
The sequence
We will pick you up directly from your hotel and your exciting journey can begin!
After a few hours in our comfortable jeeps we reach the Colored Canyon. You will love its raw beauty! You will discover its rocky formations and many spectacular colors that you would not expect in the middle of the desert. You will be accompanied by an experienced guide.
Take your time to look around, admire the breathtaking wonders of nature and take lots of pictures!
Then drive to Nuweiba by jeep for lunch. Afterwards we drive to Dahab for a walk with lots of shops - there you can buy handicrafts, carpets, papyrus and perfumes.
Then we will take you back to your hotel.
Excursion days
Every day
Pick up: 7:00 a.m.
Return: 6 p.m.
Note that the canyon tour requires some mobility and health. We don't recommend this tour for people who don't feel fit enough.

title: Grand Aquarium Hurghada

sections.Grand Aquarium Hurghada:
4 hours
Easy
Is there is anything about the Red Sea you still don't know?
The Red Sea's underwater eco-system is home to over 300 species of coral and 1,200 species of fish, 10% of which are found nowhere else in the world.
Spinner dolphins, dugongs, turtles, mantas, and sharks are just some of the marine species that calls these waters home. As a common snorkeling or diving tourist, you will never meet them all. We made for you something special - we collected the most interesting pieces from the marine life and brought them to you, to aquarium in Hurghada. Make yourself and your family special holiday day and experience "Red Sea in Glas". The visitors will find more than just an aquarium....
* Rainforest and Arabian Tent
* Mini Zoo and Fossil Museum
* Underwater tunnel
* Daily sharks feeding time - learn how aquarist feed and care for the species
The aquarium’s animals are displayed in 24 different galleries. All exhibit displays both salt and freshwater species. The other section of the aquarium is mini zoo featuring rescued animals from all over regions in Egypt. The Ocean tank contains 475,000 gallons (1,800 m3) of water and displayed several hundred of fishes. All exhibit is designed to feature the life of the Red Sea's reef system, to encourage everyone to get involved of protecting the ocean around us.
Trip includes:
Pick up from/to hotel within Hurghada
Entrance ticket to Aquarium
Please note that transfer from outside Hurghada hotels is extra charged
Drinks and refreshment
Camera

title: Jungle Aquapark

sections.Jungle Aquapark:
8 hours
Easy
Our driver will pick you up from the hotel entrance in the morning and you will be taken to the aqua park on the outskirts of Hurghada.
It's up to you how you want to spend your day! Explore the spacious park area with all its attractions and have fun with your family and friends! We are sure you cannot wait to do this! You cannot feel boring!
The amusement park, which is considered as biggest one in the area, offers a wide selection of water slides, toboggans, turbo tubes, kamikaze slides and even a swimming pool with artificial waves. And if you choose to bring your kids with you, they'll love it and they will love you for this!
There are numerous sun loungers, parasols and toilets available. Enjoy your free meal and drink at a snack bar in the aqua park at lunchtime.
The park is open from 10:00 a.m. until sunset.

Transport from your hotel to the aqua park and back
admission charge

meals in the park except the lunch

Enjoy an unforgettable day trip from Marsa Alam to Cairo by domestic flight. Accompanied by a professional English- or German-speaking guide, you will discover Egypt’s most iconic landmarks, ancient treasures, and the fascinating atmosphere of the capital city.
This excursion combines comfortable travel, rich history, and world-famous attractions in one incredible experience. Explore the legendary Pyramids of Giza, visit the famous Egyptian Museum, and enjoy panoramic views of both ancient and modern Cairo.
During the tour, you will visit the Great Pyramid of Cheops, the Pyramid of Chephren, the Pyramid of Mykerinos, the legendary Great Sphinx of Giza, and the historic Valley Temple. You will also discover the treasures of the Egyptian Museum, including the famous collection of Tutankhamun.

title: Private Tour Luxor Overnight from Hurghada

sections.Private Tour Luxor Overnight from Hurgha:
2 days
Moderate
Luxor Two days by private limousine - starting from 2 persons E
xperience the Temple of Karnak, Valley of the Kings, Hatshepsut Temple, Memnon Colossi and more ....
The sequence:
Our tour guide picks you up in the morning from your hotel entrance and you will travel to the south from Hurghada. On the way to Luxor you will pass through some of the Upper Egyptian villages and you will get an idea of life in this part of Egypt.
Karnak
First you will see the Karnak Temple - one of the most extraordinary temples in the world, as it is still in amazing condition after thousands of years.
With a little imagination you will find yourself in the past of this impressive sacred monument.
You will have plenty of time to stroll around, explore the entrance area, get a sense of how the mighty pylon was built, walk the avenue of ram-headed cribs, and discover the Hypostyle Hall with its more than a hundred huge fountains preserved columns, admire the hieroglyphs and paintings, see the sacred lake and take a look at the various chapels, temples and statues.
If you wish, approach the Sacred Scarab near the lake and surround it three times clockwise - according to legend, the descendants will soon follow!
Valley of the Kings:
Valley of the Kings, place where the ancient Egyptians buried their kings and powerful nobles over a period of 500 years, and where the mummies of some of the most famous and glamorous people of the Pharaonic era were found. You can descend to the tombs of great Egyptian rulers and discover the well-preserved colorful paintings and hieroglyphics on the walls.
Memnon
Then we'll take you to admire the 3400-year-old Colossi of Memnon, the gigantic statues of Pharaoh Amenhotep III, who once stood in front of his defunct temple complex, even larger than the Karnak Temple.
Queen Hatshepsut
After the lunch break, we are ready for another highlight - the temple of Queen Hatshepsut, who was one of the most fascinating rulers of ancient Egypt.
The Pharaoh, who demonstrated her ability to successfully rule an empire and eventually created an architectonic extraordinary mortuary temple with colonnades, a portico and many statues, paintings and reliefs.
Sound and light show
After checking in at your hotel, you can take the unique opportunity to see the fantastic "Sound and Light Show" in the Karnak Temple.
Travel back in time and enjoy with all your senses (Extra Charge).
You stay in a 4 * hotel in Luxor with half board.
Tour details - Day 2:
Today, just before sunrise, take a lifelong chance and book a flight with a hot air balloon! From the top you have a fantastic view over the famous temples, green fields and the mighty but peaceful Nile. (Supplement)
Ramses III.
After breakfast in your hotel you will have enough time to visit Medinet Habu with the great mortuary temple of Ramses III. Visiting colossal statues that adorned it as the ancient Egyptian god Osiris, well-preserved masts and walls, and more than 7,000 square meters of wall decorated with reliefs that are not only feast for the eyes, but also teach us about ancient Egyptian history, such as the victory of Ramses III. about his enemies, the "Sea Peoples," an alliance of seafarers who threatened Egypt at the time.
Nile and banana island
And finally you have the chance to take a boat trip on the Nile and sail to Banana Island, famous for its banana trees. (Supplement)
In the afternoon, it's time to leave Luxor and return to Hurghada.
Excursion days:
Everyday
Transportation surcharge price of hotels in El Gouna 5 euro per person.
Please note that the price can be reduced with 3* hotel and more people joining.
Included:
Transport in modern and air-conditioned vehicles
An English speaking guide
Entrance fees for the above attractions
Lunch on both days
1 night in a 4 * hotel in Luxor with half board
Not included:
Tipping for the tour guide and other staff
Personal expenses for meals, drinks or services, which were not mentioned above
Sound and light show in the Temple of Karnak
Hot air balloon ride over Luxor
Boat trip to Banana Island
Useful tips:
One day before your trip, you order a wake up call and Breakfast boxes at the reception of your hotel.
Protect yourself because of the high temperatures and the humidity in this part of the country (especially in the summer time) with a cap / hat and always carry enough water with you.
It is advisable to wear longer clothing or two-ply clothing since, they help to cool your body
take your passport
Since the two-day trip to Luxor is a private trip, if you would like to make changes to your daily program, please contact your tour guide at any time.

title: Overnight Cairo from Hurghada (private tour)

sections.Overnight Cairo from Hurghada (private t:
2 days
Moderate
Drive to Cairo for 2 days. See the Egyptian Museum, Pyramids, Sphinx, Khan En Khalili Bazar and Mohamed Ali Mosque.
Description:
The excursion starts around 02:00 in the morning. You will be picked up from the hotel by our guide. He will accompany you on the transfer to Cairo. The journey takes 5-6 hours.
Gizah plateau
After reaching Cairo, drive straight to Giza. There are Cheops-Chephren and Mykerinos pyramids. The largest pyramid of Cheops is one of seven wonders of the world. If you want to see pyramids from the inside, you have to pay extra admission. For a surcharge, there is also the Cheops Sun Boat Museum. With an entrance ticket you can also visit the large Sphinx. The largest statue in the world, man-made ...
After this tour we drive to the restaurant for lunch
Museum and Tutankhamun
In the afternoon you visit the largest museum in Egypt, the Egyptian Museum in Tahrir. There are more than 150,000 artifacts here. Of course, tourists are most interested in the tomb decoration of King Tutankamun. Buried with thousands of gold pieces, he got the most famous through the death mask and the sarcophagus. Extraordinary is visit of the Royal Mummy Room.
Evening program is free, you can take a small Nile boat trip or just enjoy evening Cairo. You spend the night in a 4 * hotel.
2. Day
Citadel and Bazaar
On the second day you will be picked up after breakfast. We drive to the Alabaster Mosque, you will see famous Citadel and buy traditional souvenirs in the Khan ElKhalili Bazaar. You can walk through the oriental markets. After lunch you get back on the ride to Hurghada.
Included:
Transport in a modern vehicle
German / English speaking tour guide
Hotel bed and breakfast
Entry to the sights mentioned above
Lunch on both days
Not included:
Tips for employees and travel guides
Personal expenses
Meals, drinks and services not mentioned
Felucca trip on the Nile
Dinner on a Nile ship with an oriental show
Sound and light show at the pyramids of Giza
Entrance to the pyramid, the room of the royal mummies in the Egyptian Museum
Useful tips:
Please order your breakfast box from reception the day before your trip
Supplement 10 euros for single rooms
Take your passport with you!

title: Sharm El Luli from Marsa Alam - Private Tour

sections.Sharm El Luli from Marsa Alam - Private:
1 day
Easy
Enjoy Sharm El Luli Tours and El Qulaan from Marsa Alam or Port Ghalib, a relaxing day in a natural setting!
You will be picked up from your hotel in Marsa Alam and taken to the beautiful Sharm El Luli tour. Here you will spend the day in a snorkeling zone.
Sharm El Luli is a particularly worthwhile place to explore the underwater world.
After half a day there is a snorkeling trip to El Qulaan. This is a great place to relax and swim in the sea. The water there is extremely warm and good for people with rheumatological pain. Then you can walk in the shallow water to the large mangrove tree, behind which there is a breathtaking open sea. This is a truly magical place. A world full of great landscapes and extreme serenity.
Try the original Bedouin coffee in the tent near the beach and relax for a while. The lunch served in the tent at the entrance to Qullan tastes particularly good, especially the fried fish.
Sharm El Luli Beach is ranked number 3 by the 10 best beaches in the Middle East. It is also named among 25 top beaches worldwide.

title: Horseback riding Marsa Alam

sections.Horseback riding Marsa Alam:
3 hours
Easy
Do you know that the world looks very different from the horse's back? What kind of view do you get when riding in the desert or on the edge of the Red Sea? Middle East is not just the camels, they are famous Arabian horses that have always been part of the country's coloring in Egypt.
The ancient horse culture is very much alive in Egypt, and it is doubtful that any part of the world can match its rich and broad mosaic of history.
Take this horseback tour with the finest bred horses and experience trainers. Horse trainers are also provided to give you pre-ride coaching and to assist you throughout your ride.
Arabian horses are famed for their beauty and endurance. Developed in a desert climate and prized by the nomadic Bedouin people,
they were often being brought inside the family tent for shelter and protection from theft. Selective breeding for traits including an ability to form a cooperative relationship with humans created a horse breed that is good-natured, quick to learn and willing to please.
Riding in the desert and on the beach
In our program we always offer two hours in the morning / 08-10 / during which you can stay with the horses in the desert or on the beach. You can combine them.
Horses love the sea and adore swimming, swimming with them is one of the most spiritual and beautiful connections with human
Afternoon or "sunset" programs from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm are considered photography programs.
Make this unique experience that will not only remain in vacation photos ...
Horses for everyone
Our horses are suitable for all riders. Whether you're just a beginner or a professional rider, you're sure to have fun. Of course we have the trainer with us. Be sure that our horses are well taken care of, all of them are of the highest level and people-friendly!
We have a large selection of horses and ponies.
We can adapt the riding request for professional riders (or owners of horses) and we can also take care of young customers, including beginners.
Our team also strives to keep safety, all riders are equipped with the necessary helmet.
Send us the message to receive your appointment. Children must be accompanied by an adult.
The tours are private
The tours are only suitable for limited groups. Be ready for a romantic and fantastic view.
Included in the price
2 hours of riding including the evaluation for the right horse in the stable
Refreshing water
Transfer from / to the hotel

title: Snorkeling tour to Tiran Island (Sharm el Sheikh)

sections.Snorkeling tour to Tiran Island (Sharm e:
8 hours
Easy
Snorkeling trip from Sharm El Sheikh to Tiran Island by boat, an unforgettable trip.
The sequence
Our driver will pick you up from your hotel in the morning and you will be taken to a port. You are already expected by an English speaking tour guide. You will get on a boat and your snorkeling adventure can begin!
The area around Tiran Island, part of the Ras Mohamed National Park, is home to beautiful reefs with thousands of colorful fish and other marine animals and therefore attracts many snorkeling and diving fans from all over the world.
The most famous reefs near the island are named after four English officers who discovered them: Gordon, Woodhouse, Thomas and Jackson. All but Gordon are known for their wide variety of corals and fish. During our trip we will make three stops where you will have a great opportunity to explore the wonderful underwater world of these places.
At lunchtime you can enjoy a delicious buffet on board

title: St. Catherine Monastery

sections.St. Catherine Monastery:
6 hours
Easy
You will be picked up at the hotel reception and driven directly to the monastery.
St. Catherine is at the foot of Mount Sinai, also known as Mount Moses, because Moses is said to have received the Ten Commandments here. The monastery was built on the site of the alleged real Burning Bush through which God spoke to Moses. It is believed that monastic life has existed here for over 1,600 years, making the Catherine Monastery one of the oldest monasteries in the world. Saint Helena, mother of Constantine I, the first Roman emperor who professed Christianity, ordered the construction of a chapel at the site of the Burning Bush in the early 4th century AD. on. The monastery in its current form was then built by Emperor Justinian I in the 6th century with strong fortifications to protect the monks. Thanks to these measures, its isolated location and the protection of Mohamed, the Prophet of Islam, who promised in writing that the monastery would be spared Muslim attacks, the complex remained untouched over the centuries.
Therefore, not only can you see the buildings, walls and decorations in their almost original form, countless valuable objects have been preserved to this day and are invaluable to the modern scientists who walk the paths of history. The library, for example, houses the second largest collection of ancient writings in the world, only surpassed by the Vatican library. You can also admire the world's largest collection of ancient icons, as they even survived the Byzantine iconoclasm behind the walls of the secluded monastery, destroying most of the early icons. You can also visit the various buildings and ornate mosaics, and take a look at the bush in the courtyard, which is said to be Moses' Burning Bush.
The monastery is still home to monks and has been part of the UNESCO World Heritage since 2002. As you can see now, this award is justified.
After lunch we go back to the hotel.
Included:
- Transport from the hotel and back
- Entrance fee to the monastery
- Lunch with a drink
Useful tips:
Please note that you must cover your arms, legs and head before entering the monastery.

Thanks to these measures, its isolated location and the protection of Mohamed, the Prophet of Islam, who promised in writing that the monastery would be spared Muslim attacks, the complex remained untouched over the centuries.
Therefore, not only can you see the buildings, walls and decorations in their almost original form, countless valuable objects have been preserved to this day and are invaluable to the modern scientists who walk the paths of history. The library, for example, houses the second largest collection of ancient writings in the world, only surpassed by the Vatican library. You can also admire the world's largest collection of ancient icons, as they even survived the Byzantine iconoclasm behind the walls of the secluded monastery, destroying most of the early icons. You can also visit the various buildings and ornate mosaics, and take a look at the bush in the courtyard, which is said to be Moses' Burning Bush.
The monastery is still home to monks and has been part of the UNESCO World Heritage since 2002. As you can see now, this award is justified.
After lunch we go back to the hotel.
Included:
- Transport from the hotel and back
- Entrance fee to the monastery
- Lunch with a drink
Useful tips:
Please note that you must cover your arms, legs and head before entering the monastery.

title: Ras Mohammed National Park and White Island

sections.Ras Mohammed National Park and White Isl:
8 hours
Easy
Take a trip to Ras Mohammed National Park for a relaxing boat sail and fantastic snorkeling on this full-day tour from Sharm el-Shiekh. Ras Mohamed National Park is a protected area just a short drive south of Sharm El Sheikh. Experience cruising across the Red Sea and snorkel in Ras Mohamed’s National Park crystal clear blue waters. Visit amazing White Island and encounter fish and other marine life in an underwater paradise.
Hotel pick up is around 8 am (depending on the hotel in Sharm El Sheik). We are heading to Ras Mohamed National Park, a natural protected area about 30 km south of the city. After a 20-minute drive we will reach the sea dock and join our fantastic snorkelling vessel.

title: Submarine (Sindbad)

sections.Submarine (Sindbad):
3 hours
Easy
Sindbad Submarine is the only submarine in the Middle East. This excursion is perfect for families with children and those who are not divers and want to see the underwater world of the Red Sea
The sequence
We will pick you up from your hotel and bring you to the port of Sindbad Hotel where the journey starts.
Before you go on board, spend your pre-boarding time in our small marine museum. You would be surprise how many species of fishes and water creatures are living in the sea! Don’t forget to bring your camera to capture this extraordinary adventure! When the times come, your group will head to the small catamaran boat which will bring you in the short time to the submarine in the open sea.
The vehicle has a cabin with seats and 18 round windows that allow you to watch the amazing underwater life. An experienced captain will take you to the best spots near Hurghada and due to the fact that you dive at a depth of 25 meters you can be sure that the corals are not damaged and therefore they appear in all their glory.
Outside the submarine, you can not only watch the beautiful corals. Professional divers will accompany the submarine and feed the fish right in front of your window so that you can see different types of fish up close. A spectacle for everyone!
After a 2-hour tour, we will bring you back to your hotel.
Warning: This excursion is not suitable for people suffering from claustrophobia!
Excursion days:
Every day
For the pick-up service from hotels outside of Hurghada (i.e. in Makadi Bay, El Gouna etc.) an additional fee of 5 euros per person will be charged. Added for transportation. From Safaga 10 euros.
Included:
Transport from your hotel to the port and back
Submarine Tour
Naval Museum in the port
Personal expenses for meals, drinks, or services not mentioned above
Please be informed that there is new governmental tax in place "Red Sea National Park protection tax", which is valid for all the sea trips. 1 euro p.P. are collected in the port.
Bring your camera to be astonished!

title: Turtles and Manatees - Snorkeling in Port Ghalib

sections.Turtles and Manatees - Snorkeling in Por:
8 hours
Easy
Discover one of the most exciting snorkeling experiences in Port Ghalib , located near Marsa Alam . This full-day boat trip takes you into the crystal-clear waters of the Red Sea , where vibrant coral reefs and diverse marine life create the perfect underwater paradise.
During this unforgettable adventure, you’ll enjoy two guided snorkeling stops at some of the best reef sites in the area , giving you plenty of time to explore colorful coral gardens and swim among tropical fish. One of the biggest highlights of the trip is the opportunity to swim with graceful sea turtles in their natural habitat. With a bit of luck, you may also encounter the rare and gentle dugong (Red Sea manatee) — a truly magical experience for nature lovers.
This snorkeling tour is suitable for beginners and experienced swimmers alike. Professional guides are onboard to ensure your safety and help you discover the most vibrant marine spots. Snorkeling equipment is provided, making your experience smooth and stress-free from start to finish.
Between snorkeling sessions, relax on deck and take in the stunning coastal scenery while enjoying a freshly prepared lunch served onboard , along with refreshing soft drinks. Round-trip hotel transfers are included, ensuring a comfortable and hassle-free day.
If you’re searching for the best snorkeling tour in Port Ghalib or a unique Red Sea wildlife experience near Marsa Alam, this turtles and manatees snorkeling trip offers the perfect combination of adventure, relaxation, and unforgettable marine encounters.

title: Luxor Day Trip by Plane from Sharm El Sheikh Discover Ancient Egypt in One Day

sections.About this activity:
Discover the wonders of ancient Egypt on a full-day trip to Luxor, flying comfortably from Sharm El Sheikh. This unforgettable journey takes you to the heart of Egypt’s history, where you’ll explore iconic temples, royal tombs, and legendary monuments along the Nile.
After a short morning flight, meet your expert guide and begin your adventure on the West Bank of Luxor. Visit the famous Valley of the Kings, where the tombs of powerful pharaohs are hidden deep within the desert mountains. Continue to the impressive Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, a unique architectural masterpiece carved into the cliffs, and stop at the Colossi of Memnon for memorable photos.
Cross to the East Bank and enjoy a delicious lunch at a local restaurant before exploring the vast Karnak Temple Complex, one of the largest religious sites in the world. Walk through its grand halls, towering columns, and sacred temples that tell the story of ancient Egyptian civilization.
This well-organized tour offers the perfect opportunity to experience Luxor’s highlights in just one day, combining comfort, history, and expert guidance.

title: Private Cairo Pyramids Trip from Hurghada by Minibus

sections.Private Cairo Pyramids Trip from Hurghad:
19 hours
Moderate
Enjoy a full-day private tour from Hurghada to Cairo in a comfortable air-conditioned minibus with a certified Egyptologist guide. For this long-distance journey, we use a minibus (not a small car) to ensure maximum comfort and safety.
Visit the Pyramids of Giza and the Great Sphinx of Giza, choose between the Egyptian Museum or the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM upgrade), and enjoy lunch at a local restaurant.
Pyramids of Giza – The iconic ancient tombs of the pharaohs at the Giza Plateau.
Great Sphinx – The mysterious limestone statue with a lion’s body and human face.
Egyptian Museum in Tahrir Square or Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) – Choose which museum to visit (GEM upgrade approx. €20 pp extra).
Departure: Early morning pickup (around 2:00 AM from your hotel in Hurghada) to maximize time in Cairo.
Drive: Long scenic transfer — approx. 5–6 hours each way from Hurghada to Cairo (bring snacks/comfortable clothing).
Guided Visit – Museum: Begin your day with your certified Egyptologist guide visiting the museum in Cairo.
Lunch: Enjoy a restaurant lunch in Cairo.
Guided Visit – Pyramids & Sphinx: After lunch, visit the iconic Pyramids of Giza and the majestic Great Sphinx of Giza.
Return: After sightseeing, drive back to Hurghada with an expected late evening arrival.
Pickup: ~2:00 AM (time may vary slightly by hotel location).

title: The Things You Should Avoid in Egypt

sections.The Things You Should Avoid in Egypt:
Key Things to Avoid When Visiting Egypt
Egypt offers a wealth of opportunities for exploration, culture, and memorable experiences. Known for its hospitality, the country welcomes millions of visitors each year. Egyptians are generally warm, approachable, and eager to help.
That said, like any destination, understanding local norms and avoiding certain behaviors will significantly improve your experience. Below is a concise guide to what travelers should avoid—combining practical advice with essential cultural awareness.
1. Don’t Feel Pressured by Street Vendors
Vendors are a vibrant part of Egypt’s commercial culture, especially in markets and near major attractions such as Cairo and Luxor . You may encounter persistent sales approaches, including verbal offers or following you briefly.
This is standard practice rather than aggressive behavior. If you’re not interested, a polite but firm “no” or simply ignoring the offer is sufficient. Staying calm and composed is key—over time, it becomes easier to navigate.
2. Avoid Supporting Poor Animal Welfare Practices
Camel and horse rides are commonly offered near tourist sites. However, not all operators maintain proper animal care standards.
If an animal appears exhausted, underfed, or mistreated, it’s best to decline. Supporting ethical providers not only ensures a better experience but also encourages responsible tourism. Reputable operators do exist and provide safe, respectful environments for both animals and visitors.
3. Avoid Overly Revealing Clothing
Dress expectations in Egypt vary by location. In resort areas such as the Red Sea, attire is generally more relaxed. However, in cities and public spaces, modest clothing is recommended.
As a general guideline:
Cover shoulders and knees
Apply this standard to both men and women
Follow stricter dress codes when visiting religious sites
This approach demonstrates cultural respect and helps avoid unwanted attention.
4. Respect Religious Spaces and Practices
Egypt is home to significant Islamic and Christian heritage. When visiting mosques or churches, timing and behavior matter.
Avoid visiting mosques during prayer times if you are not participating
Be mindful of ongoing services in churches
Follow entry rules and dress codes
Respectful engagement enhances both your understanding and your welcome.
5. Be Mindful When Interacting with Local Women
Cultural norms regarding gender interactions are more conservative than in many Western countries.
Avoid initiating conversations with unfamiliar women
Do not take photos without explicit permission
When interacting with families, follow their lead
In general, same-gender interactions are more common in public settings. Respecting these boundaries is essential.
6. Do Not Consume Alcohol in Public Spaces
While alcohol is available in licensed venues such as hotels and bars, public consumption is not socially accepted and may attract legal attention.
To avoid complications:
Drink only in designated establishments
Avoid carrying or consuming alcohol in the street
7. Don’t Misinterpret Local Communication Style
Egyptians are expressive and often communicate in a lively, energetic manner. Conversations may sound intense but are usually friendly.
This cultural trait can be surprising at first, but it reflects enthusiasm rather than conflict. Learning a few basic Arabic phrases can go a long way in building rapport and earning positive responses.
8. Avoid Relying on Unverified Information Sources
With the abundance of online content, misinformation about Egypt can be common—especially on social media.
For accurate details (e.g., visa requirements, entry fees, regulations):
Use official government or embassy sources
Consult reputable travel providers
Relying on verified information helps you plan confidently and avoid unnecessary confusion.
Final Thoughts
Egypt remains one of the world’s most compelling travel destinations. By approaching your visit with awareness, respect, and openness, you position yourself to fully appreciate its history, culture, and people.
Understanding both the opportunities and the boundaries ensures not just a smooth trip—but a truly enriching experience.

title: 7 Things You Can Expect in Egypt As a Traveler

sections.7 Things You Can Expect in Egypt As a Tr:
Egypt remains one of the world’s most compelling destinations—where ancient civilization meets modern complexity. For executives, planners, and discerning travelers, understanding the on-the-ground reality is key to a successful visit. Here are seven grounded insights to help set expectations and enhance the overall experience.
1. Safety: Perception vs. Reality Security is often the first concern raised when Egypt is mentioned. While the country has faced challenges in the past, the primary tourist destinations—such as Cairo, Luxor, Aswan, and the Red Sea resorts—are well-secured and closely monitored.
Travelers should expect visible security measures, including checkpoints, bag screenings, and a consistent presence of tourism police. These protocols are standard and contribute to a generally safe environment at major sites. As with any global destination, awareness and adherence to local guidance remain essential.
2. A Warm, People-Centric Culture Egyptians are widely recognized for their hospitality. Visitors frequently encounter genuine curiosity and friendliness, sometimes expressed through requests for photos or casual conversations.
While this attention can feel unexpected, it reflects a culture that values connection. In recent years, with tourism rebounding, international visitors are often welcomed with enthusiasm.
3. Cairo: Dynamic and Intense Cairo is one of the largest cities in the region, and it operates at full intensity. Traffic patterns are unconventional, streets are constantly active, and the urban rhythm is fast-paced.
For first-time visitors, the city may feel overwhelming. However, beneath the surface is a vibrant, layered metropolis rich in history, commerce, and daily life. Planning logistics carefully—especially timing visits to landmarks like the Pyramids of Giza—can significantly improve the experience, particularly during periods of low visibility due to pollution or fog.
4. A Distinctive Culinary Identity Egyptian cuisine blends Middle Eastern staples with local adaptations. Dishes such as koshary—a mix of rice, pasta, lentils, and tomato sauce—offer a uniquely Egyptian take on comfort food and remain widely accessible.
Travelers will also encounter grilled meats, spiced stews, and traditional street food. Culinary experiences are both affordable and diverse, though visitors should prioritize reputable venues to ensure quality and hygiene.
5. Tipping Culture (“Baksheesh”) Is Embedded Tipping is a deeply ingrained part of daily transactions in Egypt. From hotel staff to service attendants, small gratuities are commonly expected.
While this may feel excessive at times, the amounts are typically modest. Carrying small denominations of local currency is advisable. Understanding this cultural norm in advance helps avoid friction and ensures smoother interactions.
6. Ancient Egypt Exceeds Expectations Even seasoned travelers find Egypt’s historical sites surpass expectations. The scale, preservation, and authenticity of temples, tombs , and monuments are unmatched.
From vividly colored wall carvings to the preserved remains of pharaohs, the country offers direct access to one of humanity’s most influential civilizations. For many, this alone justifies the journey.
7. Guided Travel Enhances Value While independent travel is possible, Egypt is a destination where professional guidance significantly elevates the experience. Licensed guides provide context, historical depth, and practical navigation support.
They also assist with logistics, cultural interpretation, and managing common challenges such as persistent vendors. For executives or time-sensitive travelers, guided arrangements ensure efficiency and a more curated experience.
Final Perspective: Manage Expectations, Maximize Experience Egypt is not a standardized, Western-style destination—and that is part of its appeal. Infrastructure, service pace, and living conditions may differ from European benchmarks. Visitors may encounter visible contrasts, including urban density and economic disparity.
However, those who approach Egypt with informed expectations and cultural openness are consistently rewarded with a travel experience that is both enriching and unforgettable.
In strategic terms, Egypt is not just a destination—it is an encounter with history, resilience, and a deeply rooted national identity.

title: Egypt Visa and Airport Transfer

sections.Egypt Visa and Airport Transfer:
If you decide to fly to Egypt, you should know that the country requires an entry visa. The guests from Europe do not need to buy it in advance, it is very easy to get it directly at the airport. If you own the passport from another country / Tunis, Turkey … etc /, get information about the visa at the Egyptian Embassy. Turks living in Germany must book an Egypt trip through the travel agent.
Our advice:
Germany, Belgium, Italy, Portugal and France are the countries whose ID card is accepted for a visa. That means you do not necessarily need to have the passport for Egypt. In this case you need 2 photos (This is a rule, although it is practically only one requested). Please be careful that your documents (identity card or passport) are valid at least 6 months after the planned return flight. This is taken very seriously at your home country airport. This means that otherwise you can be already not allowed to leave from there.
After your arrival at the airport (Hurghada, Marsa Alam, Cairo …) etc. you have more options to buy the visa. Officially, you can do that at the bank right in the arrivals hall for $ 30. You wait in line, pay, get a sticker in the travel document (or on a special card, in case you have ID, not passport) and let it to be stamped in the tourist police window. You also have the possibility to pay the visa at the tour operator’s counters. It’s faster, but you pay a few dollars more.
You can officially apply for an Egyptian eVisa before you fly out. Please make sure you have enough time between filling application and the planned flight. The verification process needs few days.
Once you get the visa, it can be used for stay of 28 days. If you plan to stay longer, rent the apartment, you need to go to visa office to extend your tourist visa. Make sure you take your passport, photos and your rent contract for this.
The passport office wont deal with anyone except you. It’s a quick visit, simple when you have all needed documentation. The visa form is more of a “ purchase “ rather than an application. Don’t let lawyers or “friends” tell you you need lawyer for this. Use expats for help, they know the process.
By arrival in Sharm El-Sheikh you do not need a visa if you do not want to travel from the Sinai Peninsula. You will get for two weeks valid stamp “Sinai only” instead. If you are planning a trip outside of Sinai you will need to buy a normal tourist visa indeed. For example, you cannot travel to Cairo only with the stamp!
The transfer from the airport to the hotel can be arranged privately or through a travel agency. Private drive means you go directly to the hotel and you don’t need to make stops in other hotels and wait till the guide will assist accommodating the others. Private transfers are arranged from 10 £ per trip. Feel free to have a look on our offers. Booking is fast through whatsapp, sms or messenger. Please contact us in advance to get all the information for our service.
Before you leave the arrival hall, after passport control, you pick up your luggage. Be careful, if you miss the suitcase or have something damaged, please contact the customer service directly. You have to fill out the papers / insurance and complaint / inside the hall, once you go out, you will not be able to come back for security reasons the check. While in the arrival hall, you can always find someone who speaks English or German and get the help.
In order to connect to the world online, you will find Mobile Operator Desks / Vodafone, Orange..etc / in the arrival hall, where you may buy the Internet SIM card. You get this already activated, the prices are from 15 Euro. 20GB card costs 15 Euro, 30 GB 17 Euro, 42 GB 20 Euro (*prices are subject to change)

title: Foreigners Living in Egypt

sections.How is the life in Egypt as foreign resi:
Living in Egypt as an Expat: A Practical Overview of Cities, Lifestyle, and Key Considerations
Egypt has long been perceived primarily as a tourist destination, but in recent decades it has also become home to a growing international community. Cities such as Hurghada, Sharm El Sheikh, Cairo, Alexandria, Luxor, and Aswan now host a diverse mix of foreign residents who have chosen Egypt either temporarily or as a long-term base.
For anyone who has visited Hurghada , it quickly becomes clear that it differs significantly from a typical Egyptian urban environment. The city has developed into a highly international coastal hub, where a large proportion of residents are foreigners. Similar patterns can be observed in Sharm El Sheikh, both of which function as tourism-driven economies with strong expatriate presence. In contrast, Cairo and Alexandria offer a more traditional metropolitan experience, where foreigners are typically employed in multinational companies, diplomatic missions, education, or specialized industries. Further south, Luxor and Aswan attract a smaller but steady number of expatriates, often connected to tourism, archaeology, or cultural projects.
Across all these locations, foreign residents include retirees, remote workers, entrepreneurs, and individuals who have settled with Egyptian partners and families. In recent years, the rise of remote work has significantly increased the number of people choosing Egypt as a long-term base while maintaining income from abroad.
sections.Cost of Living and Financial Reality:
Egypt is frequently described as a low-cost destination, though the reality is more nuanced. Compared to most European cities, housing and daily expenses can be more affordable. However, prices have steadily increased over the past decade, particularly in major urban and coastal areas. As a result, Egypt is most financially attractive to foreigners with stable foreign income or savings rather than those seeking local employment.
Employment regulations can make legal work for foreigners complex, requiring permits and sponsorship. For families, education is often a key consideration. Private schools offering British, American, German, or French curricula are widely available in cities like Cairo , Alexandria, and Hurghada, though tuition fees can be significant. Most institutions operate bilingually or trilingually, with English and Arabic as the core languages.
sections.Housing and Lifestyle Choices:
Foreign residents generally choose between renting or purchasing property, depending on their long-term plans. Options range from centrally located apartments in urban areas to high-end gated communities (“compounds”) offering amenities such as private pools, security, and access to beaches.
Lifestyle differences between cities are notable. Coastal cities like Hurghada and Sharm El Sheikh offer a relaxed, resort-style environment, while Cairo presents a fast-paced, dense metropolitan lifestyle. Alexandria combines Mediterranean culture with urban living, and Upper Egypt cities such as Luxor and Aswan offer a slower, more traditional pace of life.
sections.Food, Transport, and Daily Living:
Daily living in Egypt is generally convenient and adaptable. Fresh produce, fruits, and vegetables are widely available and affordable in local markets and supermarkets. Cooking at home is cost-effective, while dining out varies significantly in price. Local restaurants offer budget-friendly meals, but international cuisine and delivery services in tourist and upscale areas can be comparatively expensive.
Transport options include private cars, ride-hailing services, and taxis. Fuel prices are relatively low by international standards, although driving conditions require adaptation due to different traffic norms and road behavior.
sections.Banking and Practical Administration:
Opening a bank account as a foreigner is possible but typically requires a valid residence visa or work permit. Procedures differ between banks, and documentation requirements may vary. Many expatriates continue to use international banking solutions alongside local accounts. Credit and debit cards are widely accepted in major supermarkets, hotels, and larger retailers, while smaller shops and local businesses generally operate on a cash basis.
ATM withdrawals using foreign cards are available, though fees depend on the issuing bank and can range significantly.
sections.Cultural Adaptation and Long-Term Living:
Egypt offers a welcoming and socially open environment, particularly in major cities and tourist regions. Egyptians are generally hospitable, but cultural and religious norms play an important role in daily life. Adaptation is essential, especially outside international or tourist-oriented areas, where lifestyle and expectations may differ more significantly.
Ultimately, living in Egypt presents both opportunities and challenges. It offers affordability, climate advantages, and a rich cultural environment, but also requires flexibility, patience, and an openness to different administrative and social systems.
For many foreigners, Egypt becomes either a long-term residence or a recurring seasonal base. For others, it remains an enriching life experience. The decision depends less on the destination itself and more on individual expectations, adaptability, and lifestyle priorities.

title: Taxi And Bus In Hurghada

sections.Taxi And Bus In Hurghada:
One of the first questions travelers ask when arriving in Egypt is how to move around the city easily and safely. Whether you want to visit local markets, shopping malls, marinas, or nearby resorts, transportation in Egypt is generally straightforward, affordable, and available at almost any hour of the day.
Fortunately, Hurghada is one of the easiest destinations in Egypt to navigate. All you need to know is where you are and where you would like to go.
sections.Exploring Hurghada by Microbus:
The most common form of public transportation in Hurghada is the white microbus. These minibuses operate throughout the city and connect popular districts such as Dahar, Sakala, El Kawther, Sheraton Road, Magawish, and many other areas.
Unlike public transportation systems in many European cities, microbuses do not follow fixed timetables. Instead, they travel along regular routes and depart once enough passengers have boarded. When you reach your destination, simply inform the driver, who will stop and allow you to get off.
Microbuses are an extremely economical way to travel, with fares typically ranging between 5 and 15 Egyptian Pounds for most city journeys.
Using a microbus, visitors can easily reach popular attractions and shopping destinations, including the Marina, local markets, the Grand Aquarium, Senzo Mall, and Hurghada City Center. If you are unsure which vehicle to take, don't hesitate to ask locals. Simply mention your destination, and someone will gladly point you in the right direction.
For many visitors, microbuses provide an authentic glimpse into daily Egyptian life. They are generally considered safe, as passengers are usually local residents commuting around the city .
sections.Taking a Taxi in Hurghada:
For travelers who prefer greater comfort and faster transportation, taxis are widely available throughout Hurghada.
The city's traditional taxis are easily recognizable by their orange and blue colors. Before starting your journey, it is advisable to note the taxi number displayed on the vehicle's door. This can be useful if you accidentally leave personal belongings behind or need to identify the vehicle later.
Although most taxis are equipped with meters, not all drivers use them consistently. Therefore, it is recommended to agree on the fare before entering the vehicle. Carrying small Egyptian Pound notes can also make payment easier and help avoid misunderstandings.
Taxis provide convenient transportation not only within Hurghada but also to nearby destinations such as Makadi Bay, Sahl Hasheesh, and El Gouna at reasonable prices.
sections.Taxi Apps: The Modern Alternative:
For those who prefer transparent pricing and cashless convenience, ride-hailing applications have become increasingly popular in Egypt.
Services such as Uber, Careem, and inDrive allow travelers to book rides directly from their smartphones. These apps display estimated fares before confirming the trip, providing peace of mind and reducing the need for fare negotiations.
They are particularly useful for visitors unfamiliar with local transportation routes or those traveling during busy hours.
sections.Transportation Available Day and Night:
One of the major advantages of transportation in Egypt is its availability. Whether it is early morning or late at night, finding a ride is rarely a problem. Public transport, taxis, and ride-hailing services operate throughout the day and night, giving visitors the flexibility to explore at their own pace.
As with travel anywhere in the world, visitors should exercise normal caution, especially when traveling late at night. Choosing reputable transportation services and sharing travel plans with companions can help ensure a smooth and enjoyable experience.
sections.Final Thoughts:
Getting around Hurghada does not need to be complicated. Whether you choose the affordable microbus, a traditional taxi, or a modern ride-hailing app, transportation is accessible, inexpensive, and available around the clock. For adventurous travelers, public transportation offers an authentic local experience, while taxis and app-based services provide comfort and convenience.
Whichever option you choose, you'll find that exploring Hurghada and its surrounding attractions is both simple and enjoyable, allowing you to focus on what matters most—making unforgettable memories in Egypt.

title: Tips and Money Exchange

sections.Tips and Money Exchange:
Tipping in Egypt: What Travelers Should Know
Tipping remains one of the most frequently asked questions among visitors to Egypt. Travelers often arrive with mixed messages—some hear that service depends entirely on extra payments in foreign currency, while others are told tipping is optional. The reality lies somewhere in between.
In Egypt’s tourism sector, tipping is both customary and appreciated. Due to the structure of local wages, gratuities often form an important part of income for service staff. For smaller services, tips typically range around the equivalent of 1 euro or 1 US dollar (approximately 50–55 Egyptian pounds). This modest amount can cover simple daily needs, such as tea at a local café or a basic breakfast.
It is also important to note that tipping is not limited to interactions with tourists; it is a common practice within everyday local life. Among Egyptians, tips may range from 20 to 100 Egyptian pounds depending on the service.
Currency and Practical Considerations
A common concern for travelers is which currency to bring. While Egyptian pounds are widely used, foreign currencies such as euros, US dollars, Swiss francs, and British pounds are generally accepted in tourist areas. However, Scottish banknotes are typically not exchangeable.
Interestingly, hotel staff often accept coins, even though banks and exchange offices do not. As a result, employees may collect small euro coins and later ask guests to exchange them for banknotes. Visitors should not be surprised if staff request such exchanges—it reflects practical limitations within the local financial system.
ATMs across Egypt dispense only local currency, typically in denominations of 50, 100, or 200 Egyptian pounds. Therefore, travelers are advised to bring small denominations (coins or low-value banknotes) from home if they intend to tip frequently.
Dining and Service Etiquette
Restaurant and bar bills in Egypt generally include taxes. While tipping is not mandatory, it is customary to leave an additional 10% for good service. This gesture is widely appreciated and reinforces positive service standards.
A Balanced Perspective
For many travelers, particularly those staying in all-inclusive resorts, tipping may seem unnecessary. However, even small gestures can have meaningful impact. Behind each service interaction is often a worker supporting a family, sometimes commuting long distances from rural areas.
Ultimately, tipping should remain a personal decision. Offering a gratuity for good service is both respectful and culturally aligned, but it should never feel obligatory. In many cases, a small tip not only enhances service but also creates a more positive and memorable experience for both guest and host.
Exchange Rates and Transactions
For accurate exchange rates, travelers are encouraged to check directly with local banks or ATMs. Online tools such as XE can also provide real-time estimates. Whether exchanging money at a bank or withdrawing from an ATM, the difference in rates is minimal, though ATMs may apply small transaction fees and round amounts to standard denominations.

title: Egyptian Cuisine - Love For The Food

sections.Egyptian Cuisine - Love For The Food:
Writing about Egypt and food is easy and difficult at the same time. Once you are here, you will find that Egyptians love food. They love to serve, and enjoy.
You will also find the simplest food that is served in "big". The tables are always full of fresh, flavored and vegetable-topped ingredients. If it's just a simple fool tameja dish or meat / lamb meal for a special occasion.
If we were to brighten up all the delicacies of Egyptian cuisine, we would need more articles….
Let us mention a few that you should definitely try if you go outside your “all-inclusive box” and are ready to try something really local
Our advice:
Koshary
We just have to start with this special “mix” food. A layer of rice, macaroni, lentils and chickpeas with caramelized onions, thick red sauce and garlic / chilli / vinegar / sauce. It may looks strange, but it tastes fantastic! If you find a real “koshary” restaurant serving it, don't hesitate and give it a try!
Mahshi
If you are on a low-fat diet, get away from it. But you will definitely be sorry that you haven't tried it!
Mahshi is basically a vegetable of choice filled with a rice filling - such as zucchini, eggplant, bell pepper, tomato, cabbage leaves or vine leaves. The filling consists of rice, herbs (parsley, coriander and dill), tomato sauce with spices and a hint of cinnamon. Once you start eating this, you won't be able to stop.
The dish is prepared in fat "Samna" butter and that's the taste!
Molokheja
To remember this meal, you will call it "Egyptian Spinach". It is a leafy green stew, cut into small pieces and prepared in chicken, beef or seafood soup mixed with ground cilantro and fried garlic (we also mix in chopped tomatoes).
You can only eat it with Egyptian bread alone or you can have it served with boiled rabbit and vermicelli rice.
Fattah
Fattah goes back to ancient Egypt, where it was served in large celebrations. Fattah is still a main course on the first day of the Islamic festival (Eid al-Adha). It is a combination of crispy bread, meat and rice in tomato sauce.
Lamb is branded on the festive days. Otherwise you will love the beef.
Shawerma
Shawerma (chicken or beef) can be served as a dish, but most likely you will taste it as a sandwich first.
It originally came from the famous Greek gyros, but the Egyptians have taken their turn.
The meat is scraped off and placed in packaging. It is typically served with vegetables, tahini and the chicken with tomaya (garlic sauce).
Macaroni bechamel
There is no Egyptian family where this dish would not be served regularly!
Made from Rigatoni pasta, minced meat, cooked in chopped onions and tomato paste, topped with the classic Bechamel sauce. Simple but tasty!
Pigeon stuffed with rice
This is more restaurant than home cooking. Pigeon filled with flavored rice, vegetables and lots of sauces. Are you ready to try? Sometimes you can find it in your hotel restaurant for dinner.
Desserts and sweets
When Egyptians eat something sweet, it is really "sweet". You can try the famous Kunafa or Basbousa dessert. Do you just like " Roz Bel Laban" for breakfast - Egyptian version of rice pudding?
You can satisfy your sweet hunger with the bowl "Om Ali". Legend has it that Sultan Ezz El Din Aybak's wife turned this dish into a victory celebration where it was made and shared among the people of the country. Since then it has become famous after her.
Dessert combined with sweet flavors of bread (puff pastry), milk and sugar - the Egyptian version of bread pudding. It is usually topped with a mixture of raisins, nuts and pieces of coconut, served hot.
Is there any way to warm up on the windy Egyptian night, sit in the café and ask for "Sahlab". Its base is hot milk, mixed with pure orchid root powder, which gives it a tasty and thick taste. Sahlab can be conveniently made with instant sachets that contain this powder and other ingredients such as vanilla, sugar and cinnamon.
Are you ready? Let the food adventure begin!

title: The Egyptian Money - Do You Recognize It?

sections.The Egyptian Money - Do You Recognize It:
Every banknote - special symbol King Chephren shows a 10 pound banknote with Horus, who protects him, one of the most beautiful statues exhibited in the Egyptian Museum . Chephren ordered his sculptors to carve the god Horus behind his head, which means that he represented Horus on earth.
LE 20 banknote refers to Pharaoh warrior Ramses III. who went into battle with "seafarers" in his horse-drawn carriage. This is the famous scene that can be seen in his mortuary temple Medinet Habu .
The chariot is a two-wheeled vehicle that was used for racing and fighting in ancient times and was pulled by a horse or horses. It was built in the 16th century BC. Brought to Egypt by the Hyksos invaders.
50 Egyptian pound (0.8 GBP, 1 USD) banknote refers to Temple of Edfu. You will probably visit this part of Egypt on your Nile cruise on the way to Asuan . The Horus Temple in Edfu, also known as the Edfu Temple, is said to be the best preserved and second largest temple in Egypt after the Karnak Temple. The Temple of Horus reflects the traditional Pharaonic architecture that was used during the Ptolemaic era from 237 to 57 BC. Chr. While built It was oriented from east to west instead of the current north-south configuration.

title: Security and Traveling in Egypt

sections.Security and Traveling in Egypt:
"Is it safe to travel to Egypt when I'm just a woman alone?" "Can we travel and feel safe when we have young children?" "Is it really that I shouldn't be leaving my hotel? What about a walk on the street? ”
Yes, the question of safety is important these days. No wonder people abroad often ask this question.
Our advice:
Egypt as a travel destination is not more dangerous than other travel destinations are. The country's government is also concerned about your security. It endeavors to protect the places where you are. It begins with the hotel security staff, continues with tourist police officers in the entrance area and on the streets. Ends with the police convoys organized when traveling to places like Abu Simbel or Western Desert. To be honest, police are everywhere in Egypt. However, this does not mean that you can only stay at the hotel resort and should not travel to explore the country yourself.
Hurghada, Sharm El Sheikh, Marsa Alam and other cities are tourist friendly. There is no need to worry even for women traveling alone, unless some rules should be followed. Then you can feel free and safe at the same time.
If you want to travel in the tourist center, try public transport (microbus, tok-tok, street taxi, bus). If you prefer private transportation , there are more "limousine" companies that give you deals straight from your hotel. They will pick you up at the hotel reception and bring you back there after the trip.
The transportation price is on a good budget (for Europeans). You can travel from city to city, but if you are a larger group of foreigners, your bus should travel under a tourist license - with the driver and guide with permission to work with tourists. Here you should know that an Egyptian citizen is not allowed to relocate tourists if he is not registered with the authorities. This is one of the points regarding your safety.
In order to enter certain places in the country (western desert, area near the Sudan border, near the Israel border, etc.) you need a special permit from the national security office.
Please note that small excursions into the desert / jeeps / quads / vehicles must be registered for tourist purposes and the distance to the city is limited. The boats that offer sea trips - diving , snorkeling , have to register their movement regularly. Night trips and tracks on tourist buses (between 6:00 p.m. and 6:00 a.m.) are monitored and registered by the Ministry of Tourism and the Tourist Police. There are "checkpoints" - small police stations across the country to check travelers in vehicles (be careful, taking photos of army or police objects is prohibited)
If you only need a simple taxi or bus transportation near your hotel, please see our next article…. Coming soon ... Travel and survival on public transport ...
Are you still concerned about security and movement in Egypt? May you be impressed by Gallup's new data study for 2019, which shows Egypt is number 8 on the list of the safest countries in the world. It is the second safest country in the Middle East.
